jobs.title: Clinical Development Risk & Compliance Manager
jobs.description:
When our values align, there s no limit to what we can achieve.   At Parexel, we all share the same goal - to improve the world s health. From clinical trials to regulatory, consulting, and market access, every clinical development solution we provide is underpinned by something special - a deep conviction in what we do.
 Each of us, no matter what we do at Parexel, contributes to the development of a therapy that ultimately will benefit a patient. We take our work personally, we do it with empathy and we re committed to making a difference.
 
 
 Key Accountabilities :
 Lead, facilitate and support the risk management process for assigned projects/Partnership/accounts using the available tools.
 Work with other RCLs to oversee and direct the risk management process across multiple projects within the account.
 Lead cross-functional teams, functional leaders, and subject matter experts (SMEs) in identifying, prioritizing, and mitigating risks, embedding solutions into project strategies.
 Evaluate and refine mitigation effectiveness, driving continuous improvement in risk management practices.
 Establish best practices for documenting and disseminating lessons learned, fostering a culture of knowledge-sharing.
 Provide expert guidance to other RCLs and serve as a resource for resolving escalated risk challenges.
 
 Lead proactive analysis of key risk indicators and signals across projects and accounts, through Quality Risk Evaluations (QRE), leveraging dashboards, metrics and predictive tools 
 Synthesize complex data into actionable insights, presenting high-level risk assessments and recommendations to project teams and leadership 
 Direct the development and implementation of mitigation strategies, ensuring their long-term success and alignment with organizational goals.
 Mentor other RCLs on QRE process and consult leadership on escalated risks
 Serve as SME for QRE as assigned by LM.
 
 Serve as a senior consultant on complex consultancy requests and SOP deviations, drawing on deep knowledge of ICH GCP, Parexel processes, regulatory requirements, and applicable country standards 
 Provide strategic, real-time advice to project teams and leadership to ensure first-time quality and resolve high-impact challenges.
 Lead investigations into project-level SOP deviations (across projects and accounts), collaborating with SMEs to recommend solutions
 
 Partner with project teams to sustain an audit/inspection-ready state, providing advanced oversight of storyboards and documentation practices. Assist with audit and inspection preparations, including prioritizing tasks and reviewing critical documents 
 Coach teams on maintaining audit/inspection readiness as a proactive, ongoing priority. Proactively identify gaps in documentation encouraging project teams to document via project-level story boards where applicable
 Provide senior-level support during audits/inspections, may be required to host audit/inspection and manage high-stakes engagements with confidence and expertise
 
 Serve as the Quality Representative for Quality Event (QE) – Quality Issues and Audit/Inspection Findings 
 Triage quality issues in electronic quality management system (eQMS) setting standards for accuracy and consistency. 
 Support in the initial assessment of Serious Breach of GCP and coordinate swift and effective containment strategies with the QE team 
 Drive Root Cause Analysis (RCA) efforts with established methodologies, ensuring the development and execution of impactful Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPAs) 
 Assess and enhance CAPA effectiveness, providing strategic direction to optimize quality outcome 
 Review and refine audit/inspection responses prior to final QA submission ensuring alignment with quality standards 
 Mentor other RCLs on audit/inspection excellence as Quality Representative
 
 Exemplify adherence to company standards
 Exemplify adherence to company standards, ensuring timely completion of training, timesheets, expense reports, and CV updates per SOPs.
 Maintain rigorous compliance with ICH-GCP, company processes, and global regulatory requirements.
 Ensure seamless continuity of responsibilities during absences, coordinating with leadership for oversight 
 
 Mentorship and Team Support
 Mentor other RCLs and serve as an integration partner to facilitate induction into the CDRCM team.
 
 Customer Interactions and Relationship Management 
 Build and maintain strategic relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including senior client representatives and third-party vendors, to align risk and compliance priorities. 
 When assigned the role of Account Risk and Compliance Lead (ARCL): Lead quality-to-quality meetings as needed
 Perform account-level risk and issue management
 Provide account-level quality oversight, serving as a point of escalation and communicating pertinent information to account leadership as necessary 
 Develop or revise quality agreements as required
 
 
 Champion innovative improvement in identifying gaps, leading initiatives, and influencing organizational strategy
 Drive innovation by proposing and implementing advanced solutions to CDRCM leadership, enhancing departmental efficiency and standards.
 Review and provide feedback on SOPs as assigned.
 
 Skills: 
 Outstanding interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills with proven ability to influence senior stakeholders, lead discussions, and mentor diverse teams.
 Exceptional relationship-building capabilities, fostering trust and collaboration with internal teams, clients and external partners.
 Advanced proficiency in synthesizing complex datasets to identify trends, mitigate risks, and inform strategic decisions.
 Expert analytical skills, excelling in risk identification, root cause analysis, and strategic evaluation of interventions.
 Highly adaptable, with a demonstrated ability to lead through ambiguity, apply critical thinking, and drive change in dynamic environments.
 Masterful in planning, prioritizing, and managing complex workloads independently, with a relentless focus on patient and client outcomes.
 Compliance- and risk-focused, with meticulous attention to detail, ownership of outcomes, and a proactive leadership approach.
 Experienced in navigating and leading within matrix environments, inspiring teamwork and delivering results across functions.
 Willingness to travel 15% - 20%. 
 
 Knowledge and Experience : 
 8- 12 years’ experience in clinical research 
 Demonstrated experience in Quality or Risk Management activities 
 Expertise in root cause methodologies and CAPA development 
 Proven leadership in mentoring teams and managing complex, multi-project accounts 
 Comprehensive mastery of ICH-GCP principles and their strategic application. 
 Fluency in written and oral English; additional languages an asset. 
 
 Education: 
 Bachelor’s Degree required preferably in life sciences or health-related discipline or equivalent experience.
 Advanced degree (master’s degree) in a science, technology or industry-related discipline strongly preferred.

jobs.title: Principal Compliance Consultant (Former FDA ORA/OMQ Experience Preferred)
jobs.description:
When our values align, there s no limit to what we can achieve.   At Parexel, we all share the same goal - to improve the world s health. From clinical trials to regulatory, consulting, and market access, every clinical development solution we provide is underpinned by something special - a deep conviction in what we do.
 
 Each of us, no matter what we do at Parexel, contributes to the development of a therapy that ultimately will benefit a patient. We take our work personally, we do it with empathy and we re committed to making a difference.
 
 
 An incredible opportunity to apply your FDA experience in a way that drives solutions and meaningful impact for clients.
 
 What if your next role allowed you to move beyond observations and actively shape outcomes, influencing compliance strategies and driving lasting improvement across the industry?
 
 At Parexel Consulting, former FDA professionals partner with industry to solve complex compliance challenges, guide strategy, and drive meaningful, lasting improvements across global life sciences organizations.
 
 Parexel Consulting is growing and we are seeking a Principal Consultant with prior FDA experience to join our Strategic Compliance team. This role is designed for senior professionals who have built deep expertise within the FDA and are now interested in applying that experience in a more proactive, solution-oriented environment.
 
 In contrast to a strictly enforcement-focused role, this position provides the opportunity to work directly with clients to identify risks early, solve complex regulatory challenges, and help organizations achieve and sustain compliance—ultimately impacting product quality and patient outcomes.
 
 This opportunity offers a unique transition from regulatory enforcement into strategic advisory work, where you will:
 Move beyond identifying observations to helping clients prevent them
 Apply your FDA experience to shape compliance strategies before inspections occur
 Partner with organizations to resolve complex issues rather than only documenting them
 Influence outcomes by guiding remediation, readiness, and long-term quality improvements
 Work across a diverse set of companies, products, and global environments
 
 Key Responsibilities
 Serve as a senior advisor to clients on FDA regulatory expectations, inspection strategy, and compliance risk mitigation
 Lead inspection readiness and mock inspection programs, helping clients prepare with confidence
 Apply firsthand FDA knowledge to anticipate inspection findings and proactively address gaps
 Advise on and support response strategies for:
 Form FDA 483 observations
 Warning letters
 Import alerts
 Regulatory meetings
 Consent decrees and enforcement actions
 
 Guide clients through root cause analysis, remediation planning, and sustainable compliance solutions
 Provide expertise in risk-based decision-making, including regulatory discretion and supply continuity
 Collaborate across cross-functional teams to support complex quality and regulatory engagements
 Mentor team members and contribute to building internal expertise
 Engage in client discussions and contribute to business development efforts
 
 Experience Required
 A proven track record of experience working at the FDA in the Office of Regulatory Affairs (ORA), Office of Inspections and Investigations (OII), and/or Office of Compliance (CDER)
 Leadership in domestic and international GMP inspections, including foreign cadre assignments
 Experience conducting for-cause, pre-approval, and surveillance inspections
 Involvement in high-priority or complex inspections
 Direct experience supporting regulatory enforcement actions, including:
 Drafting or contributing to Warning Letters
 Developing Import Alert recommendations
 Participating in recalls, regulatory meetings, or enforcement escalations
 
 Engagement with industry on compliance expectations and post-inspection follow-up
 
 Preferred Technical Expertise
 Data Integrity inspections and remediation
 Sterile manufacturing / aseptic processing
 API and drug product manufacturing across multiple dosage forms
 
 Qualifications
 Prior experience with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration in inspection, compliance, or enforcement roles
 Demonstrated experience across the inspection and enforcement lifecycle
 Strong understanding of GMP compliance and pharmaceutical manufacturing systems
 Experience conducting inspections internationally or as part of a foreign inspection cadre
 Ability to translate regulatory expectations into clear, actionable solutions for clients
 Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
 Ability to travel 50-70% with a focus on international travel
 
 Education & Experience Requirements
 Bachelor’s degree required (life sciences, engineering, public health, or related field)
 Advanced degree preferred (e.g., MPH, MS, PhD, MBA)
 15 years of related experience, including significant FDA experience in inspection, compliance, or enforcement roles
 
 What We Offer
 Opportunity to move from enforcement to influence, helping organizations proactively meet regulatory expectations
 Exposure to complex and high-impact challenges across global life sciences clients
 Collaborative consulting environment with leadership and mentorship opportunities
 The ability to directly contribute to improving product quality, compliance, and patient safety
 
 #LI-LB1
 
 EEO Disclaimer Parexel is an equal opportunity employer.  Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to legally protected status, which in the US includes race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status.

jobs.title: Sr Sales Incentive Compensation & Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
Sr Sales Incentive Compensation & Financial Analyst 
 Syneos Health® is a leading fully-integrated life sciences services organization built to accelerate customer success. We partner with innovators at every point across the drug development and commercialization continuum, helping them navigate complexity, anticipate change and accelerate progress.   Every day we perform better because of how we work together, as one team, each the best at what we do. We bring together talented experts across a broad spectrum of business critical corporate functions. Every role plays an essential part in enabling our customers to achieve their goals. Our teams are agile, collaborative, and committed to delivering—for each other, for our customers, and ultimately for the people who rely on the services we support. Discover what your 25,000 future colleagues already know:   Why Syneos Health • We are passionate about developing our people, through career development and progression; supportive and engaged line management; technical and therapeutic area training; peer recognition and total rewards program. • We are committed to building an inclusive culture – where you can authentically be yourself. Central to this is our purpose – Driven to Deliver – which captures the passion of our colleagues to show up each day and shape solutions that have the ability to dramatically impact someone’s life.  • We are continuously building the company we all want to work for and our customers want to work with. Why? Because we know that when we bring together smart colleagues from across the world, we can shape the future of healthcare, driving impact for customers and defining the pace of patient progress. 
 
 Job Responsibilities 
 
 Job Summary   
 The   S enior S ale s   Incentive Compensation   &   Financial   Analyst supports   the end-to-end administration of sales incentive compensation   and commission   programs. This role   is responsible for   ensuring   data accuracy,   supporting   commission calculations   and analysis,   administration   and   inquiry   resolution ,   while   partnering closely with Sales Operations   and   Business Development.   Additional   responsibilities include providing professional and/or technical level support and guidance.   The ideal candidate is   highly   analytical ,   detail-oriented, and comfortable working   with autonomy   in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment .   This position requires working a U.S.-aligned schedule ending at 2:00 AM local IST time, providing overlap with U.S. business hours. 
   
 Primary Responsibilities   
 Assist   with commission plan modeling   and analysis.   
 
 Procure   and   validate   Salesforce CRM   dat a   used   in   commission calculations.   
 
 Support the commission calculation process , including p erform ing   quality   controls   to ensure   accuracy .   
 
 Assist   with drafting,   issuing   and   maintaining   commission plan   documents   and sales enablement materials .   
 
 Prepare and distribute commission statements   to participants.   
 
 Triage ,   research , and resolve   commission inquiries; escalate complex issues as   appropriate .   
 
 S upport ad hoc analysis requests   including   development of   summary materials, insights to support leadership decision making, and   e xecutive-ready outputs.   
 
 Support   territory reassignments   and target updates   impacting   commission calculations .   
 
   
 Additional Responsibilities   
 Participate in   additional   projects and initiatives as assigned.   
 
   
 Qualifications   
 Bachelor’s degree in Business , Finance, or   a   related   field.   
 
 Minimum   of   two   ( 2 )   years of   experience   in Incentive Compensation, Finance Operations, or a   comparable   analytical role.   
 
 Proficiency   with Microsoft Office applications (Word, PowerPoint, Outlook ). Advanced   Excel   skills   required , including complex   formulas and   pivot tables . Must have the ability to   quickly and independently build, validate, and maintain complex commission models and analyses with a high degree of accuracy . Exposure to m acro s   and   Power   BI   preferred .   
 
 0 -2 years Salesforce experience   preferred .   
 
 Ability to develop professionally formatted summary materials   and analytical outputs .   
 
 Experience   supporting multi-region   commission or incentive compensation   programs   a plus .   
 
 Strong data and financial analysis skills .   
 
 Ability to   identify   and understand broad-scope issues while   maintaining   strong attention to   detail with a ccuracy .   
 
 O rganizational skills   with the   ability to   manage competing priorities and   meet tight deadlines.   
 
 Proactive,   problem-solving   mindset with   sound judgment and   the ability to   make recommendations .   
 
 Strong team   orientation with   excellent follow-up and   internal   stakeholder support .   
 
 Excellent verbal and written communication skills   with a collaborative , partnership-focused approach.   
 
 
 Get to know Syneos Health 
 
 Over the past 5 years, we have worked with 94% of all Novel FDA Approved Drugs, 95% of EMA Authorized Products and over 200 Studies across 73,000 Sites and 675,000 Trial patients.
 
 No matter what your role is, you’ll take the initiative and challenge the status quo with us in a highly competitive and ever-changing environment. Learn more about Syneos Health.  
 http://www.syneoshealth.com 
 
 Additional Information 
 
 Tasks, duties, and responsibilities as listed in this job description are not exhaustive.  The Company, at its sole discretion and with no prior notice, may assign other tasks, duties, and job responsibilities. Equivalent experience, skills, and/or education will also be considered so qualifications of incumbents may differ from those listed in the Job Description. The Company, at its sole discretion, will determine what constitutes as equivalent to the qualifications described above.   Further, nothing contained herein should be construed to create an employment contract.  Occasionally, required skills/experiences for jobs are expressed in brief terms. Any language contained herein is intended to fully comply with all obligations imposed by the legislation of each country in which it operates, including the implementation of the EU Equality Directive, in relation to the recruitment and employment of its employees.  The Company is committed to compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, including the provision of reasonable accommodations, when appropriate, to assist employees or applicants to perform the essential functions of the job.

jobs.title: Senior Legal Operations Analyst
jobs.description:
GROWING WHAT MATTERS STARTS WITH YOU 
 
 As the world’s only major agriscience company completely dedicated to agriculture, we’re building a culture that stays curious, thinks differently, acts boldly, and takes a stand on what’s right for our customers, our co-workers, our partners and our planet. We know we’ve got big challenges to solve - we hope you ll be part of the solution. Working at Corteva connects you with more than 20,000 colleagues united by a shared vision to grow what matters. We offer career opportunities across more than 140 world-class R&D facilities and in more than 130 countries. 
 
 We’re hiring for Senior Analyst to join our Legal team!
 
 Learn how you can be our voice in the conversation about the future of agriculture.
 
 You Will Be Part of Growing Team 
 
 This position is on the Legal Operations team and supports legal function global processes, application administration, data management, installation support, user support, end user training, technology assistance, program coordination, and incident solution for the Corteva Legal function.  Responsible for ensuring systems are maintained, data is properly updated, integrity of data and applications, and resolve issues. The candidate will work closely with Legal team members, IT, and vendor team members in ensuring successful user experience of legal applications.  There will also be interactions with Procurement, R&D, Sales, Finance, and Human Resources.
 Legal applications that will be used include: Onit, NetDocuments, Symphony, hCue, LRN (iComply), CaseIQ, Truyo, Navex Global (Risk Rate), Power-BI, and SharePoint sites.
 
 The role will be performed within the frame of Corteva’ s Brand values: 
 
 Job responsibilities 
 System Administration for all Legal applications (installation, accesses, and training)
 Managing and tracking system integrations.
 Application reporting including user metrics.
 Managing application upgrades, data loads, maintenance, and testing.
 Running scripts, scheduling tasks and monitoring jobs for applications.
 Updating workflows in various legal applications.
 Updating clause and template libraries for contract management application.
 Updating privacy system templates.
 Application data auditing to ensure data integrity.
 Resolve issues, identify patterns, and recommend enhancements
 Escalation to other individuals on Legal Ops team or IT when needed
 
 Location: 
 
 Corteva Global Service Center, Hyderabad, India 
 
 To Grow What Matters, You Will Need: 
 
 Qualification: Bachelor’s degree in information technology or a related field.
 Years of experience: 4-6 in System or Application Administration or equivalent.
 Strong experience working with legal applications such as matter management, e-billing, and/or document management.
 Strong knowledge and 2-3 years of experience working in Legal Operations is a plus.
 Must have prior experience supporting and / or administrating applications (legal applications is a plus).
 Must be an experienced problem solver with strong analytical skills.
 Must have a strong application and workflow logic understanding.
 Must be a flexible team player with the ability to work independently.
 Must be a collaborative team player with a demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally
 Must have excellent written and oral communication skills; ability to understand complex business analysis and specifications and communicate complex ideas clearly
 Proven relationship builder with good interpersonal skills.
 Ability to learn quickly and take leadership of project tasks.
 Strong planning and organization skills.
 Strong attention to detail.
 Strong skills in requirements gathering for application enhancements.
 Ability to effectively prioritize and juggle multiple concurrent priorities.
 Strong capabilities in the usage of Microsoft Office 365 tools: Excel (reporting, pivot tables, arrays)
 Power-BI (building reports and metrics dashboards for leadership)
 Outlook, Teams (communication, collaboration)
 Word (documentation)
 SharePoint (create / design, manage security, collaborate, integrate charts and tables)
 
 Understanding of IT Architecture, Databases, and Applications.
 Knowledge of PowerShell, Power-BI, Scripting and Automation tools is a plus.
 Experience with Legal Operations processes is a plus.
 Proficiency in document management systems and legal enterprise software is a plus.
 Experience in the contract field, contracts administration, or contract management is a plus
 Experience with legal analytics and legal terminology is a plus
 Experience in website design is a plus.
 
 Who Are We Looking For? 
 Curious, bold thinkers who want to grow their careers and be part of a winning team
 Market shaping individuals who want to transform the agriculture industry to meet the world’s growing need for food
 Collaborators who thrive in a diverse, inclusive work environment
 Innovators who bring initiative and fresh ideas that drive our business into the future and make us an industry leader
 
 GROWING WHAT MATTERS STARTS WITH YOU… WHAT CAN WE OFFER TO HELP YOU GROW? 
 Opportunity to be part of a global industry leader working to discover solutions to the most pressing agricultural challenges of our time
 Challenging work assignments that grow your skills, capabilities and experiences
 Diverse, inclusive work environment where employees bring their whole selves to work and feel heard, valued and empowered
 Dedicated and customized resources help grow your professional skills, industry expertise and personal perspectives.
 Opportunity to strengthen your professional network through valuable relationships.
 Support for the health and well-being of every employee by offering world-class benefits, meaningful work and competitive salary
 Performance driven culture with a strong focus on speed, accountability and agility

jobs.title: Lead Business Process Analyst
jobs.description:
About the Role
 
 We are seeking a Lead Business Process Analyst to join our Analytics & Digital Solutions (ADS) with a strong focus on Operations Business Leadership (OBL) priorities. This role will involve working closely with Logistics, Planning, Commercial, Customer Service and in this role, you will lead initiatives that deliver scalable, action-oriented analytics solutions.
 You will partner closely OBL and data engineering teams to co-create high-quality analytics products, support design reviews, and enable structured deployments. As a functional analyst, you will also drive adoption, change management, and sustained utilization of logistics analytics solutions. This role supports global and regional logistics operations by delivering data-driven insights and analytics products that improve process efficiency, service performance, cost optimization, and continuous improvement, with solutions designed to scale across regions where applicable.
 
 What’s in it For You • The opportunity to take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best • This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly recognized global growth company offering competitive compensation and benefits in addition to career growth
 
 Key Responsibilities
 · Partner with OBL to translate OBL priorities into analytics-enabled process improvements
 · Facilitate problem definition, business framing, and outcome alignment with senior stakeholders
 · Collaborate with global and regional leaders to standardize and scale logistics analytics solutions
 · Act as a business process expert and contribute to knowledge management, documentation, and continuous improvement
 · Stay current on industry and analytics trends to identify opportunities for new technologies and practices
 · Act as the functional SME for logistics reporting and analytics tools, enabling faster insights and improved effectiveness
 · Ensure solutions are usable, embedded in operations, and delivering measurable impact
 · Support process efficiency, automation, and controls initiatives across logistics operations to improve operations
 · Identify process, service, and cost improvement opportunities, including AI-enabled use cases
 · Co-develop AI-ready analytical data models with data engineering and operations teams to enable action-oriented logistics insights
 · Design and deliver reporting for logistics KPIs, including transportation, service, cost, and execution performance
 · Ensure data availability, reliability, quality, validation, and reconciliation across all logistics analytics products
 · Lead design reviews, functional QA, and governance for dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions
 · Identify logistics and transportation process, service and cost improvement opportunities, including AI-enabled use cases
 · Ideate, prototype, and iterate solutions using analytical tools such as Power BI, python etc. balancing analytical rigor with usability
 · Plan and support analytics deployments, including documentation and delivery readiness
 · Own adoption strategy, change management, and value realization for analytics solutions
 · Stay current on operations, supply chain, and analytics trends to identify improvement opportunities
 · Ability to manage multiple, complex initiatives in a fast-paced environment
 · Mentor and develop associates to independently lead projects, strengthen business process expertise, and deliver measurable operational outcomes
 
 Minimum Qualifications
 · Bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, supply chain, or a related field
 · 10 years of experience in analytics, logistics, transportation, supply chain, or business process roles
 · Good SAP experience in Logistics function
 · Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with strong attention to detail
 · Proficiency in Power BI and working knowledge of SQL, Python, Azure and Snowflake
 · Strong communication, organization, and collaboration skills
 · Proven ability to drive change management and value realization through analytics solutions
 · Ability to manage multiple initiatives in a fast-paced environment
 · Experience working in Warehouse Management System (WMS) and Transportation Management System (TMS)
 · Working knowledge of ETL processes and data warehousing
 
 Preferred Qualifications
 · Experience working with SAP logistics, transportation and warehousing
 · Experience working with Transportation management systems such as Blue Yonder, Mercury Gate etc will be added advantage
 · Experience working with Warehouse management systems such as Körber etc will be added advantage
 · Advanced degree or certification in supply chain, logistics, analytics, or computer science such as ASCM CLTD etc
 · Team management experience will be an added advantage

jobs.title: Product Enablement & Support Manager - Business Intelligence
jobs.description:
At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, delivering critical insights and innovative solutions to help our customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments. With our worldwide reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to own your future and impact what matters. Are you ready to make an impact?
 
 What’s in it For You: 
 You will join a growth company offering a competitive salary and benefits. 
 The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
 The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.
 Feel proud each day to work for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments.
 
 Job Overview: 
 
 The Manager, Business Intelligence Product Enablement & Support is responsible for leading the global support and enablement operations for Ecolab’s Business Intelligence (BI) products. This role partners closely with Business Intelligence Demand, Product Development, and Data teams to ensure BI products are reliable, issues are resolved efficiently, and users are effectively enabled to leverage analytics to drive business outcomes.
 This role focuses on running the operations behind BI support, including monitoring, issue intake and triage, troubleshooting, stakeholder communications, and the establishment of a scalable, globally consistent BI support operating model.
 
 What You Will Do: 
 Responsibility for Business Intelligence platforms and products supporting global business activities across the enterprise
 Serve as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and BI technical resources for product support, issue resolution, and user enablement.
 Lead issue intake, triage, and resolution activities, including monitoring, prioritization, escalation, and coordination across BI, data, and platform teams
 Ensure BI platforms and products are properly supported and meet or exceed defined SLAs, including required reporting to leadership and ownership of critical break‑fix events
 Drive collaboration and communication between Business Intelligence Demand, Product, Development, and Data teams in support of platform stability, adoption, and continuous improvement
 Establish, document, and scale a globally consistent BI support operating model, including intake channels, triage standards, escalation paths, communication patterns, and service expectations
 Partner with delivery and data (IT) teams to identify recurring issues, drive root cause analysis, and improve overall product quality and reliability.‑cause analysis, and improve overall product quality and reliability
 Lead enablement activities, including knowledge articles, user guidance, and support documentation, to reduce recurring issues and improve self-service adoption‑service adoption
 Identify, onboard, and supervise the resources necessary to support BI platforms, including analysts, engineers, vendors, and contractor resources as needed
 
 Minimum Qualifications: 
 Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (Analytics, Information Systems, Computer Science, Business, or Finance)
 8 years of professional experience
 5 years of experience supporting or operating Business Intelligence, analytics, or data platforms
 3 years of experience managing teams or leading operational support functions
 Experience working with third-party support teams and vendors‑party support teams and vendors
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 Strong proficiency in SQL, including data validation, reconciliation, and root cause investigation cause investigation‑cause investigation
 Strong proficiency in Power BI, including troubleshooting reports, datasets, refresh failures, access issues, and performance concerns
 Strong proficiency in Snowflake, including investigating data issues and understanding upstream and downstream dependencies
 Proficiency in Databricks, including understanding how notebooks, jobs, or pipelines impact BI datasets and user-facing reporting‑facing reporting
 Experience supporting enterprise analytics or reporting platforms at scale
 Experience with ServiceNow and ITIL-based incident and problem management principles‑based incident and problem management principles
 Experience building or scaling support operating models across distributed teams
 Strong communication, interpersonal, and people leadership skills, with the ability to build effective, cohesive, and collaborative cross functional teams‑functional teams
 Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail ‑solving skills and attention to detail
 Knowledge of application and data platform concepts such as security, databases, cloud infrastructure, and data pipelines

jobs.title: Sr Project Engineer (Analytics)
jobs.description:
Position summary At Ecolab, we grow by delivering measurable outcomes—helping teams perform at their best while protecting what’s vital. This role builds and sustains an operational “cockpit” that connects engineering activity, operational performance, and basic financials into a clear, trusted set of KPIs and Power BI dashboards. You will translate data into insights, enabling leaders to focus on what matters most, simplify decision-making, and drive consistent execution.
 What you will do
 Collect and structure data: Identify, access, and consolidate data from engineering and business sources (e.g., ERP/finance extracts, timesheets, CMMS/EAM, project tools, spreadsheets), ensuring quality, traceability, and reliable refresh.
 Build scalable reporting models: Create and maintain clean data models and measures (Power Query, DAX) so KPI definitions are consistent, auditable, and reusable.
 Define KPIs that drive action: Partner with engineering, operations, and finance to define and document KPIs across activity levels, performance, and financials (targets, thresholds, and drivers), creating a clear KPI dictionary and “single source of truth.”
 Design the cockpit in Power BI: Build intuitive dashboards and scorecards (executive summary and operational drill-down) with trends, targets, variance to plan, and exception views that help leaders prioritize and remove noise.
 Enable a disciplined reporting cadence: Deliver weekly/monthly performance reporting and automated refresh schedules; provide clear commentary on key movements, exceptions, and required follow-ups.
 Collaborate as One Ecolab: Work cross-functionally to validate logic, resolve data gaps, and align the cockpit to the decisions leaders need to make.
 Strengthen governance and trust: Maintain documentation for KPI definitions, calculation logic, data lineage, and change control to reduce “number debates” and improve decision speed.
 Continuously improve and simplify: Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting, improve data capture, and enhance the cockpit as priorities evolve.
 Minimum qualifications
 Bachelor’s degree with 3 to 5 years of experience
 Candidates must be excellent in Power BI and Power Query, Good at MS SQL.
 Hands-on experience building dashboards in Power BI (Power Query, data modeling, DAX measures).
 Strong analytical problem-solving skills; ability to reconcile data, explain variances, and build confidence in the numbers.
 Advanced Excel capability; working knowledge of SQL (or equivalent) for querying and validation.
 Working understanding of engineering and/or operations performance concepts (e.g., throughput, backlog, cycle time, downtime, productivity, utilization) and comfort engaging with technical stakeholders.
 Ability to interpret basic financials (cost categories, budget vs. actuals, forecasts, simple P&L drivers) and connect financial outcomes to operational drivers.
 Clear communication skills—able to translate business questions into KPI definitions and simple, decision-ready visuals.
 Demonstrated ownership, attention to detail, and ability to deliver on a recurring reporting rhythm.
 Preferred qualifications
 Experience with modern data platforms (Snowflake data warehouse/lake concepts, ETL/ELT) and/or Azure data services.
 Power BI administration experience (workspaces, deployment, row-level security, governance).
 Exposure to ERP and finance systems; understanding of cost accounting concepts relevant to engineering (e.g., maintenance cost, project cost, capex/opex).
 Experience building executive scorecards and operational drill-down reporting that supports business reviews.
 Lean/Continuous Improvement, reliability, or performance management exposure.

jobs.title: Sr Supply Chain Analytics Analyst
jobs.description:
JOB TITLE
 Sr. Supply Chain Analyst (Developer Data Analyst) – Procurement Logistics
 Job Description          You see things a little differently. So do we. Our Analytics & Digital team (ADS) is responsible for driving innovation via automation, strategic business insights and process optimization.    Ecolab Company has an opening for a Logistics Data Engineer in our Procurement Analytics and Digital Solutions group located in Pune, IN.  If you are a passionate professional that is seeking opportunity, advancement, and a rewarding career we invite you to apply. This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly recognized global growth company offering competitive compensation and benefits in addition to career growth.     The individual will work with the Digital Capabilities team and will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining data pipelines and infrastructure to support our logistics procurement analytics operations. This role will involve working closely with cross-functional teams to ensure data accuracy, reliability, and availability for analysis and reporting purposes. The ideal candidate should possess strong technical skills and a passion for working with data. 
 What’s in it For You:  •    The opportunity to take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments.  •    The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.  •    The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.  •    This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly recognized global growth company offering competitive compensation and benefits in addition to career growth. 
 Job Overview
 We are seeking a talented and experienced Logistics Data Engineer to join our team. The Logistics Data Engineer will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining data pipelines and infrastructure to support our logistics analytics operations. This role will involve working closely with cross-functional teams to ensure data accuracy, reliability, and availability for analysis and reporting purposes. Key Responsibilities •    Develop and maintain new data pipelines using Azure Data Factory: Design, build, and maintain scalable and efficient data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into our data warehouse. This includes handling large volumes of structured and unstructured data related to logistics operations.  •    Data modeling and architecture: Collaborate with stakeholders to understand logistics data requirements and design efficient data models and architecture that support analytics and reporting needs. Ensure data integrity, accuracy, and consistency throughout the data lifecycle.  •    Data quality and validation: Implement data validation processes and quality checks to identify and resolve data anomalies, inconsistencies, and errors. Work closely with business users to understand data issues and provide timely resolutions.  •    Data integration and API development: Integrate data from external systems, vendors, and partners through APIs or other data integration methods. Develop and maintain data interfaces to enable seamless data exchange between systems.  •    Performance optimization: Identify and implement strategies to optimize data processing and query performance to ensure timely data availability for analytics and reporting purposes. Monitor and fine-tune the performance of data pipelines to maintain efficiency and reliability.  •    Documentation and knowledge sharing: Document data engineering processes, data models, and technical specifications. Share knowledge and best practices with the team to foster a collaborative and data-driven culture.  •    Collaboration and cross-functional support: Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including logistics operations, business analysts, data scientists, and IT, to understand their data needs and provide technical support and guidance. Participate in project discussions and contribute to data-driven decision-making processes. Lead the development of new data products empowering the building of new Logistics Analytics. Qualifications •    Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or a related field. Advanced degree is a plus.  •    Proven 2 years of experience as a Data Engineer, preferably in a logistics or supply chain environment.  •    Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, SQL. Experience with data integration tools and frameworks is desirable.  •    Exposure to Azure / AWS / FIVETRAN cloud service platforms.  •    Exposure to Snowflake •    Exposure to SAP tables and logistic module •    Proficiency in data modeling and database design principles.   •    Familiarity with ETL processes, data warehousing concepts, and business intelligence tools.  •    Understanding of logistics operations, supply chain management, and related data domains.  •    Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.  •    Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with cross-functional teams.  •    Ability to adapt to a fast-paced environment and handle multiple projects simultaneously.

jobs.title: Lead, HR Quality and Compliance
jobs.description:
Lead, HR Quality and Compliance
 Location: Hyderabad
 
 This is an office‑first role. Working together in person supports close, real‑time collaboration and team alignment, enabling us to deliver our best work. You will work primarily in the office, with the flexibility to work remotely for 40% of your time (two days per week)
 
 Intro summary of the role 
 This role is responsible for ensuring full statutory and regulatory compliance across HR, Payroll, and Talent Acquisition functions. The individual will proactively monitor legislative changes, implement necessary process updates, and partner with Legal, Finance, and HR Operations teams to manage compliance risk. The role also drives continuous improvement of payroll and TA processes while supporting audits, system enhancements, and key performance reporting. Strong stakeholder management and ownership of complex compliance issues are central to this position
 
 What you will get 
 An agile career and dynamic working culture.
 An inclusive and ethical workplace.
 Compensation programs that recognize high performance.
 A variety of benefits dependent on role and location.
 The full list of our global benefits can be found here: https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits .
 
 What you will do 
 To ensure compliance with all statutory requirements, financial regulations or any other instructions in regard to HR, Payroll and Talent Acquisition (where applicable).
 Identify and implement changes to ensure that the Talent Acquisition and Payroll service is compliant, to current legislation, financial regulations, company policy and Labour Law and Agreements (CBA) as applicable to role
 Proactively monitor compliance changes in respective countries and provide compliance updates to HR Operations & delivery teams, stakeholders and create/deliver communication plan to update impacted parties liaising with Lonza Legal team where required
 Whilst ensuring compliance and management of risk to always have the workforce experience at the heart of processes
 Ensure deadlines are met for statutory returns and payments where applicable.
 Continuous improvement of payroll related processes & controls including business continuity plans, approaches to delivering a modern payroll function, simplification of legacy approaches.
 Continuous improvement of talent acquisition processes & controls including business continuity plans, approaches to delivering a modern talent acquisition function, simplification of legacy approaches.
 Work closely with finance colleagues for monthly and period payroll-related activities including reconciliations, maintenance of control accounts as applicable within HR, payroll and Talent Acquisition.
 Leading audits that impact HR Operations and Delivery with support from wider team
 Manage ad-hoc projects.
 Take ownership of complex and technical issues and queries related to compliance and risk control.
 Advise the team and wider stakeholders on options and risks for a range of business decisions from a compliance and risk capacity perspective related to change.
 Cultivate relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
 To gather, analyse and provide the relevant details in support of performance indicators and targets as and when required  i.e. TA/WFA/payroll performance quality and accuracy
 To provide appropriate management information as and when required.
 To assist in the development and implementation of TA, HR & Pay systems and their longer-term development as required to support simplification of compliance and risk control.
 To ensure periodic statutory deductions from pay are properly reconciled and returns completed by the due date.
 Interacts and works closely with other HR Operations teams and the wider HR Community to deliver best in practice services to the Business and GPO.
 
 What we are looking for 
 3 years of experience in HR, Payroll, or Talent Acquisition compliance, with a strong understanding of statutory requirements, financial regulations, and Labour Law/CBA.
 Proven experience identifying and implementing compliance changes in line with evolving legislation and company policy.
 Experience ensuring statutory returns, payments, and periodic deductions are reconciled and submitted within deadlines.
 Strong process improvement mindset, with experience supporting continuous improvement, business continuity planning, and system simplification initiatives.
 Experience supporting or leading audits, with the ability to take ownership of complex, technical compliance and risk-related issues.
 Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to advise teams and partners on compliance and risk implications, while maintaining a workforce-first approach.
 
 About Lonza 
 At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30 sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
 
 Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
 
 Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

jobs.description:

 Intro Summary of the Role

jobs.title: Senior Analyst - Agentforce
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
#LI-Onsite Location: Hyderabad, India We are looking for a Senior Analyst - Salesforce Agentforce to design, build, and support AI-powered agents on the Salesforce platform. The role will primarily focus on Agentforce agent configuration, prompt building, Salesforce development, and Apex-based customizations. The ideal candidate should have hands-on experience with Salesforce development, strong understanding of Apex, and practical exposure to building or configuring AI agents using Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein AI, and prompt engineering concepts.  
 Job Description 
 Key Responsibilities  
 Design, configure, and maintain Salesforce Agentforce agents  
 Configure agents using Agentforce Builder including topics, actions, instructions, and escalation paths  
 Understanding of different LLMs, tools calling and basic functions  
 Build and manage agent topics, actions, instructions, and guardrails  
 Build custom Agentforce actions using Salesforce Flow, Apex, and external APIs  
 Create and optimize prompts for agent responses and business workflows  
 Create and maintain prompts using Salesforce Prompt Builder  
 Develop custom Salesforce functionality using Apex, SOQL, and Lightning Web Components  
 Build and expose business logic through Apex classes, triggers, and REST APIs  
 Integrate Agentforce agents with Salesforce data, flows, Apex actions, and external systems  
 Ground agent responses using Salesforce data, knowledge articles, Data Cloud, or approved business sources  
 Configure and support Einstein AI features within Salesforce  
 Work with business teams to understand use cases and convert them into agent capabilities  
 Work with admins, developers, and business users to convert business processes into agent-driven workflows  
 Build integrations using REST APIs, Platform Events, Named Credentials, and External Services  
 Support testing, debugging, deployment, and monitoring of Salesforce and Agentforce solutions  
 Test agent responses for accuracy, relevance, compliance, and business alignment  
 Monitor agent performance, conversation quality, and error patterns  
 Implement secure agent access using Salesforce permissions, sharing rules, field-level security, and data access controls  
 Follow Salesforce security best practices including profiles, permission sets, sharing rules, and data access controls  
 Document agent configurations, prompts, Apex logic, and integration designs  
 
 Essential Requirements 
 4 years of experience in Salesforce development  
 Certifications Mandatory: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist  
 Experience in building and deploying enterprise grade Agentforce agents  
 Hands-on experience with Apex, SOQL and triggers  
 Experience with Lightning Web Components  
 Experience with Salesforce Flow, especially auto-launched flows used for automation and agent actions  
 Hands-on experience with Salesforce Agentforce or Einstein Copilot  
 Exposure to Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein Copilot, or Salesforce AI features  
 Experience with Salesforce Prompt Builder or prompt engineering concepts  
 Ability to configure Agentforce topics, actions, instructions, and grounding  
 Experience creating Invocable Apex methods and Apex actions for Flow or Agentforce  
 Experience integrating Salesforce with external systems using REST APIs  
 Experience with Named Credentials, connected apps, and secure API callouts  
 Good understanding of Salesforce security, including permission sets, sharing rules, and field-level security  
 Experience with deployment tools such as Salesforce CLI, change sets or DevOps pipelines  
 Strong debugging, problem-solving, and communication skills  
  
 Skills Desired 
Advertising Campaigns, Alteryx, Analytical Thinking, Brand Awareness, Business Networking, Curiosity, Digital Marketing, Marketing Communications, Marketing Plans, Marketing Strategy, Media Campaigns, Process Documentation, Statistical Analysis, Strategic Marketing

jobs.title: Sr. Associate, Content Ops Analytics
jobs.description:
Career Category 
Sales & Marketing Operations 
 Job Description 
 Position Overview 
 The Senior Associate, Content Data Analyst supports metadata tagging operations and content ecosystem reporting for Global Content Strategy. This role focuses on internal operations, helping ensure the accuracy, consistency, and performance of metadata tagging while enabling visibility into CCEL (cross channel engagement leads) operational performance through dashboards and reporting.
 The role requires strong analytical skills, hands‑on experience with reporting tools, and the ability to work with structured content data from AEM and related Adobe platforms. The analyst partners closely with India‑based content teams to support tagging quality, operational efficiency, and reporting reliability.
 
 Key Responsibilities 
 1. Metadata & Tagging Analytics 
 Extract, analyze, and validate metadata and tagging datasets from AEM and related systems.
 Identify patterns, inconsistencies, and gaps in tagging to support operational improvements.
 Support tagging audits through data validation, metric tracking, and reporting.
 Monitor and improve metadata accuracy, completeness, and consistency across assets.
 
 2. CCEL Operations Performance & Reporting 
 Develop and maintain dashboards to track CCEL operational performance, including throughput, turnaround time, and efficiency metrics.
 Provide regular reporting on tagging operations, highlighting bottlenecks and optimization opportunities.
 Support operational reviews by delivering accurate and timely data insights.
 
 3. Measurement, Data Quality & Visualization 
 Build and maintain dashboards using Power BI and develop queries using SQL/Python.
 Ensure structured, clean, and reliable datasets for reporting and analysis.
 Establish and monitor data quality checks and validation processes.
 Identify opportunities to automate reporting processes to improve accuracy, consistency, and efficiency.
 Document metric definitions and maintain consistency across reports.
 Support continuous improvement initiatives aligned with business objectives.
 Define and document standardized metric definitions and reporting methodologies.
 
 4. Tools & Systems 
 Strong proficiency in Power BI, Excel, and SQL.
 Working knowledge of Python or similar tools for data validation and automation.
 Experience with AEM and Adobe content ecosystem tools preferred.
 
 5. Stakeholder Collaboration 
 Partner with India-based content production and tagging teams for operational reporting needs.
 Collaborate with U.S. metadata and content strategy teams to ensure alignment in reporting.
 Communicate insights clearly to support decision-making and operational improvements.
 
 Qualifications & Skills 
 Required: 
 Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Analytics, Information Management, or related field.
 5-9 years of experience in data analytics, preferably in content or digital environments.
 Strong skills in Qlinksense, Power BI, SQL, Excel, and basic Python.
 Experience working with metadata, tagging frameworks, or content management systems.
 Strong attention to detail and data quality.
 Preferred: 
 Experience with AEM or Adobe Experience Cloud tools.
 Familiarity with content operations or marketing workflows.
 Ability to work in a fast-paced, operational environment with multiple stakeholders.
 
 
 
 
.

jobs.title: Principal RWE Scientific Research Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
The Principal Real-World Evidence (RWE) Research Analyst is responsible for the scientific and methodological aspects of all RWE projects as well as providing guidance for other members of the team.  
 Job Description 
 Key Responsibilities : 
 Produce analytic deliverables, including full study reports for RWE or observational database analyses projects.  
 Support project management by coordinating project activities and timelines as well as issue management.    Independently draft and edit documents such as high-level research proposals, protocols, and statistical analysis plans.  
 Develop project timelines together with the Real-World Evidence (RWE) Data Scientists.  Appropriately track communications with the customer as well as project related decisions taken.  
 Provide guidance to less experienced analysts on study design and analysis activities.  
 Conduct observational data analyses involving new creative approaches and oversee data management and statistical programming activities.  
 Provide guidance to conduct data quality reviews with detailed documentation.    Present research and analysis results to customers and stakeholders.  
 Produce analysis datasets, listings, tables, and figures for research projects, according to specifications, while maintaining documentation and complying with pre-defined project / study standards.  
 Perform in-depth research and quantitative and qualitative analysis independently. Provide guidance to Associate RWE Research Analysts and RWE Research Analysts.    Seek out opportunities for the development of new RWE services and new customers within Novartis.  
 Maintain familiarity with technical developments in RWE, epidemiological and data science fields.  Support in talent identification and advancement as well as hiring of new talent and team expansion strategies.  
 Develop department-level standards, tools, and templates.    Contributor to RWDA business development.  
 
 
 Essential Requirement : 
 
 Experience in the application of statistical methods to the analysis of observational data. 
 Technical proficiency in analytical and visualization tools and statistical programming languages such as SQL, SAS, R, R/shiny, Tableau, Python. 
 Deep knowledge of RWE data sources and standards such as CPRD, JMDC, Optum, Healthverity, PharMetrics, OMOP.  
 Expert in applied statistics. Extensive experience in the application of statistical methods for analysis of observational data including propensity scores, sensitivity analyses, etc. is a plus.  
 Good understanding of organizational processes. Extensive experience working cross-functionally with key internal stakeholders.  
 Open to experimentation and taking smart risks to support creative thinking that leads to practical solutions to healthcare and business challenges.  
 Holds a high standard on quality excellence. Continuously seeking to enhancing standards, technology through expansion of knowledge and training.  
 Support teamwork to swiftly and efficiently deliver innovative new products to patients and healthcare providers.  
 High ethical values and standards.  
 Able to speak out, challenge conventional thinking, and stand up for ideas.  
 Experienced in data visualization.  
 Excellent project management skills: can prioritize multiple tasks and goals to ensure timely completion.  
 Confident and competent when interacting with internal stakeholders.  
 Strong written/verbal communication skills. Highly effective at summarizing and presenting key considerations and evidence.   
 Strong team spirit.  
 Preferred Postgraduate qualification (Master’s or PhD) in a relevant field.  
 Preferred Publications or presentations in recognized RWE/biomedical journals/conferences highly desired.  
 
 
 Desirable Requirement : 
 
 Bachelor’s degree plus 8 years conducting research in the pharma industry, contract research organization, or academic institute; or experience in a closely related discipline within the pharma industry (e.g., clinical research, statistics, epidemiology, pricing).  
 
 Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion 
 We are committed to building an outstanding, inclusive work environment and diverse teams representative of the patients and communities we serve. 
   
 Accessibility and accommodation 
 Novartis is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to individuals with disabilities. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the recruitment process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please send an e-mail to diversityandincl.india novartis.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information. Please include the job requisition number in your message. 
  
 Skills Desired 
Building Constructions, Clinical Research, Clinical Trials, Drug Development, Hazard Identification (HAZID), Health Sciences, Immunology, Intensive Care UnIT (Icu) (Inactive), Internal Controls, Internal Medicine, Internet Protocol Suite, Job Description (Inactive), Medical Information, Organizing, Patient Care, Stakeholder Engagement, Utilization Management (Um) (Inactive)

jobs.title: Senior Analyst - Agentforce
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary 
We are looking for a Senior Analyst to design, develop, and maintain full-stack web applications on AWS while contributing to AI agent development on the Salesforce Agentforce platform. The primary focus is building Agentforce agent facing backend services, frontend interfaces, data pipelines, and API integrations that support marketing cloud intelligence use cases. The candidate should be comfortable using AI-assisted development tools and have basic familiarity with Salesforce development.  
 Job Description 
 Key Responsibilities 
 Location – Hyderabad #LI Hybrid 
 Build and maintain backend APIs and services using Python (Django preferred)   
 Develop responsive frontend interfaces using React or similar JavaScript frameworks   
 Design and manage data pipelines for ingestion, transformation, and analytics   
 Implement caching, session management, and real-time data patterns using Redis   
 Deploy and operate services on AWS (ECS/Fargate, Lambda, S3, RDS, SQS, CloudWatch)   
 Set up and maintain CI/CD pipelines, containerized deployments, and infrastructure as code   
 Write automated tests and maintain code quality standards   
 Use AI coding tools to accelerate development while ensuring correctness and security   
 Support the design and configuration of Agentforce agents including sub-agents, actions, and instructions   
 Assist in building integrations between Agentforce agents and enterprise systems using REST APIs and Platform Events   . Document systems, APIs, and agent configurations   
   
 Essential Requirements: 
 4 years of experience in full-stack application development. Strong proficiency in Python and JavaScript   . Experience with Django framework   
 Familiarity with React or similar frontend frameworks   . Hands-on experience with Redis for caching, queuing, or pub/sub patterns   
 Working knowledge of AWS core services (compute, storage, database, messaging, security)   
 Experience with relational databases (PostgreSQL preferred) and SQL    . Familiarity with Docker, Git, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure as code (Terraform or CDK)   
 Comfort using AI-assisted coding tools in day-to-day work   
 Experience with asynchronous job processing (Celery, SQS, Step Functions)   . Strong problem-solving, debugging, and communication skills   
 Exposure to Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein AI features, or prompt engineering concepts   
 Familiarity with LLM integration patterns (prompting, tool calling, RAG)  . 
 
 Desirable Requirements: 
 Observability and monitoring best practices (logging, tracing, alerting)   . Experience in regulated industries (pharma, healthcare)   
 Salesforce certifications (Platform Developer I/II, AI Associate, AI Specialist) are a plus  . 
 Salesforce Agentforce, Einstein AI, prompt engineering, Strong proficiency in Python,  AWS CDK and components, serverless or containerized deployment patterns, experience in deploying large scale applications on AWS

jobs.title: Senior Customer Strategy Analyst
jobs.description:
When our values align, there s no limit to what we can achieve.   At Parexel, we all share the same goal - to improve the world s health. From clinical trials to regulatory, consulting, and market access, every clinical development solution we provide is underpinned by something special - a deep conviction in what we do.
 Each of us, no matter what we do at Parexel, contributes to the development of a therapy that ultimately will benefit a patient. We take our work personally, we do it with empathy and we re committed to making a difference.
 
 
 The Customer Strategy Senior Analyst in Access will be a vital member of the Customer Access Strategy team, enabling and supporting the development of proposals and account plans. The individual is highly motivated, able to work independently, and thrive in a dynamic fast-paced environment.  Ideally, the candidate will have experience in developing solutions or proposal strategies for new business opportunities in a CRO or Service Consultancy/Agency.
 
 The Customer Strategy Senior Analyst will support multi-disciplinary teams to create solution strategies and market-leading service proposals that delight our clients and ultimately help to bring new medicines to patients. They work in partnership with the Customer Strategy Sr Director as well as Subject Matter Experts (SME) in Parexel Access Consulting. The Customer Strategy Senior Analyst will support with the tracking of new business activities and contracting to ensure timely systems updates and execution.
 
 Key Accountabilities  
 Proposal Research and Development: 
 
 Support proposal development and authoring, budgeting and proactively engage experts across Parexel to create market access/commercialization solutions that maximize value for the client and differentiate Parexel from its competitors
 Facilitate strategy discussions and drive the development of client-specific, competitive, and profitable solutions, scope, and budget
 Work with Access SMEs and Service Line leads to developing account strategies and expanding relationships
 Support Access SMEs in researching leads (i.e. sponsors), their assets and positioning the Parexel Access solution appropriately for client needs.
 Leverage public sources and subscription databases, such as Citeline, Data monitor, in developing an Access point of view for client discussions, proposals and bid defense discussions.
 Work with Access Operations team to identify resource needs for proposals and awarded projects
 Actively seek to expand knowledge of industry trends and innovations, relevant products, and pipelines, and identify future business opportunities
 
 Business Development and Proposal Management :
 
 Ensure client responses reflect strategy discussions, meet client needs (relevance and appropriateness), offer consistent messaging, and convey value proposition and critical success factors
 Support in bid pursuit meetings as needed (e.g., drive thoughtful approach to content generation and Subject Matter Expert identification)
 Prepare budgets/ Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) in collaboration with Senior Vice President Access, SMEs, Service Line leadership and as needed Ops and Finance
 Support in tracking New Business Awards (NBA) and ensuring data is up to date
 
 Account and Solutions Expansion: 
 
 Work with SVP Access and Access SMEs to develop and drive content to assist in early engagement conversations and proposals
 Maintain a strong consultative “outside-in”/One Parexel approach in developing unique business solutions for clients and in driving industry leadership
 Maintain awareness of key competitors and industry trends to improve and evolve our Access and Commercial consulting offerings
 
 Qualification 
 Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred
 
 Knowledge/Skills 
 Fluent English, verbal and written
 4 years of relevant experience in a market access or life sciences Service Consultancy/Agency is required
 Comfort with desk research and synthesis of findings
 Some relevant knowledge of product development, or drug development (bench to patient)
 Make it happen attitude, capable of thriving in a complex matrix environment with an ability to build strong cross functional relationships
 Demonstrated ability to follow directions and prioritize workload; can collaborate with peers and work effectively in a team to achieve desired outcomes
 Demonstrated ability to communicate ideas in a simple, understandable, and compelling manner; basic understanding of commercial needs and objectives, with respect to supporting service proposal and pricing activities; proficient in MS Word and PowerPoint.

jobs.title: SSU & Regulatory Coordinator (Contract Management Assistant)
jobs.description:
SSU & Regulatory Coordinator (Contract Management Assistant) 
 Syneos Health® is a leading fully-integrated life sciences services organization built to accelerate customer success. We partner with innovators at every point across the drug development and commercialization continuum, helping them navigate complexity, anticipate change and accelerate progress.   Our Clinical Solutions team members act with a drug development mindset, applying their years of experience and deep expertise to truly understand customer needs and represent those in the solutions we shape.   Whether you join us in a Functional Service Provider partnership or a Full-Service environment, you’ll collaborate with passionate problem solvers, innovating as a team to help our customers achieve their goals. We are agile and driven to deliver – for one another, our customers, and, most importantly, for those in need.   Discover what your 25,000 future colleagues already know:   Why Syneos Health • We are passionate about developing our people, through career development and progression; supportive and engaged line management; technical and therapeutic area training; peer recognition and total rewards program. • We are committed to building an inclusive culture – where you can authentically be yourself. Central to this is our purpose – Driven to Deliver – which captures the passion of our colleagues to show up each day and shape solutions that have the ability to dramatically impact someone’s life.  • We are continuously building the company we all want to work for and our customers want to work with. Why? Because we know that when we bring together smart colleagues from across the world, we can shape the future of healthcare, driving impact for customers and defining the pace of patient progress. 
 
 Job Responsibilities 
 
 Responsible for providing assistance in the delivery of Site Start-Up (SSU) activities in one or more of the following functional areas: investigator site contracts, ethics and regulatory submissions, and essential documents required for site initiation. Support projects within Clinical Operations on a global scale, with oversight from the SSU Country Manager and Central and Shared Services leadership. Ensures regulatory and site contract documentation complies with sponsor and Company requirements. Works with internal and external team members to implement solutions for project and/or individual site contract related problems. Establishes strong working relationships with customers, internal project teams and sites. Ensures all relevant documents are submitted to the Trial Master File (TMF) as per Company Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/Sponsor requirements. 
 
 Get to know Syneos Health 
 Over the past 5 years, we have worked with 94% of all Novel FDA Approved Drugs, 95% of EMA Authorized Products and over 200 Studies across 73,000 Sites and 675,000 Trial patients.
 
 No matter what your role is, you’ll take the initiative and challenge the status quo with us in a highly competitive and ever-changing environment. Learn more about Syneos Health.
 
 http://www.syneoshealth.com 
 
 Additional Information 
 Tasks, duties, and responsibilities as listed in this job description are not exhaustive.  The Company, at its sole discretion and with no prior notice, may assign other tasks, duties, and job responsibilities. Equivalent experience, skills, and/or education will also be considered so qualifications of incumbents may differ from those listed in the Job Description.  The Company, at its sole discretion, will determine what constitutes as equivalent to the qualifications described above.   Further, nothing contained herein should be construed to create an employment contract.  Occasionally, required skills/experiences for jobs are expressed in brief terms.  Any language contained herein is intended to fully comply with all obligations imposed by the legislation of each country in which it operates, including the implementation of the EU Equality Directive, in relation to the recruitment and employment of its employees.  The Company is committed to compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, including the provision of reasonable accommodations, when appropriate, to assist employees or applicants to perform the essential functions of the job.

AML fraud management involves detecting, preventing, and managing money laundering and financial fraud. It includes monitoring transactions, identifying suspicious activities, and ensuring compliance with laws and regulations to protect financial institutions and their customers from fraudulent activities. Fraud Detection, Prevention, Investigation, Sanction Screening & understanding of financial regulations.
Understand & implement laws, and regulations designed to stop the practice of generating income through illegal actions during transfer of funds. Anti Money Laundering law covers limited number of transactions and criminal behaviors to prevent terrorist financing and money laundering and compliance programs, including know-your-customer (KYC) rules. Key tasks involve Customer Identity verification, Risk Assessment & Screening, Compliance & regulatory adherance, alert managment & escalation and data management & reporting

• Please note that this role may require you to work in rotational shifts This includes driving customer identification, customer due diligence & enhanced due diligence. •Knowledge of banking products like Insurance, Annuities, Mortgages, loans, credit cards etc.

AML fraud management involves detecting, preventing, and managing money laundering and financial fraud. It includes monitoring transactions, identifying suspicious activities, and ensuring compliance with laws and regulations to protect financial institutions and their customers from fraudulent activities. Fraud Detection, Prevention, Investigation, Sanction Screening & understanding of financial regulations.
Understand & implement laws, and regulations designed to stop the practice of generating income through illegal actions during transfer of funds. Anti Money Laundering law covers limited number of transactions and criminal behaviors to prevent terrorist financing and money laundering and compliance programs, including know-your-customer (KYC) rules. What are we looking for? •Written and verbal communication
•Adaptable and flexible
•Collaboration and interpersonal skills
•Ability to work well in a team Roles and Responsibilities: •In this role you are required to solve routine problems, largely through precedent and referral to general guidelines
• Your expected interactions are within your own team and direct supervisor
• You will be provided detailed to moderate level of instruction on daily work tasks and detailed instruction on new assignments
• The decisions that you make would impact your own work

• Please note that this role may require you to work in rotational shifts

Help clients transform their compliance function from reactive to proactive through an intelligent compliance operating model powered by data, intelligent technologies and talent
AML fraud management involves detecting, preventing, and managing money laundering and financial fraud. It includes monitoring transactions, identifying suspicious activities, and ensuring compliance with laws and regulations to protect financial institutions and their customers from fraudulent activities. What are we looking for? •Written and verbal communication

• The decisions that you make would impact your own work

• Please note that this role may require you to work in rotational shifts 
 
 
Any Graduation

Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps the world’s leading businesses, governments and other organizations build their digital core, optimize their operations, accelerate revenue growth and enhance citizen services—creating tangible value at speed and scale.
