jobs.title: Analyst II
jobs.description:
By clicking the “Apply” button, I understand that my employment application process with Takeda will commence and that the information I provide in my application will be processed in line with Takeda’s Privacy Notice and Terms of Use .  I further attest that all information I submit in my employment application is true to the best of my knowledge.
 
 
 Job Description 
 
 The Future Begins Here 
 At Takeda, we are leading digital evolution and global transformation. By building innovative solutions and future-ready capabilities, we are meeting the need of patients, our people, and the planet. 
 Bengaluru, the city, which is India’s epicenter of Innovation, has been selected to be home to Takeda’s recently launched Innovation Capability Center. We invite you to join our digital transformation journey. In this role, you will have the opportunity to boost your skills and become the heart of an innovative engine that is contributing to global impact and improvement.   
 At Takeda’s ICC we Unite in Diversity 
 Takeda is committed to creating an inclusive and collaborative workplace, where individuals are recognized for their backgrounds and abilities they bring to our company. We are continuously improving our collaborators journey in Takeda, and we welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Here, you will feel welcomed, respected, and valued as an important contributor to our diverse team 
 
 OBJECTIVES / PURPOSE
 We are seeking a strong Senior Analyst to join Takeda s GCC Commercial Insights & Analytics (I&A) organization in India. This role carries significant individual analytical ownership, bridging execution-level work with emerging stakeholder-facing responsibilities. The Senior Analyst is a critical analytical contributor who drives the quality and depth of insights delivered to US brand teams and begins to shape the analytical direction of assigned workstreams.
 Own and independently deliver a portfolio of mid-to-high complexity analytical workstreams that directly inform US brand strategy, performance management, and commercial execution across assigned brands and indications.
 Apply advanced proficiency in US pharmaceutical commercial data sources — including patient-level claims, EMR, specialty pharmacy, and sales data — and analytics tools to generate decision-ready insights with clear business implications.
 Design and execute patient journey analyses, HCP segmentation models, performance deep-dives, and competitive assessments that provide actionable, evidence-based recommendations to US I&A brand leads and commercial partners.
 Serve as an emerging analytical thought leader within the GCC team — contributing to methodology development, analytical best practices, and the informal mentoring of Analyst colleagues.
 ACCOUNTABILITIES
 Brand Analytics and Insight Generation
 Own end-to-end delivery of assigned performance analytics workstreams, producing reports and dashboards that track brand KPIs — including patient shares, new starts, source of business, adherence, and HCP engagement — at national and subnational levels.
 Integrate and analyze diverse US commercial data sources — including real-world data (claims, EMR), specialty pharmacy data, sales data, and channel data — to generate comprehensive patient and HCP insights that support brand strategy and launch readiness.
 Develop and maintain business rules for processing raw patient-level data, leveraging a deep understanding of data nuances, commercial context, and market knowledge to ensure analytical accuracy and consistency.
 Lead custom reporting and complex ad hoc analysis requests with independent hypothesis formulation, rigorous methodology, and well-documented, insight-rich outputs.
 Data Visualization and Reporting Excellence
 Design and maintain Tableau or Power BI dashboards and reporting tools that clearly communicate brand performance trends, patient funnel dynamics, and field force activity to US stakeholders.
 Establish KPIs and metrics — including patient shares, duration of therapy (DoT), adherence rates, source of business, and campaign effectiveness — and monitor their performance across national and subnational geographies and payer channel types.
 Proactively identify opportunities to automate and scale recurring reporting workflows using SQL, Python, or Databricks, reducing manual effort and improving analytical reproducibility.
 Stakeholder Collaboration and Communication
 Partner with GCC Managers and US I&A brand leads to clarify analytical requirements, align on deliverable scope, and communicate progress proactively.
 Synthesize findings from multiple data sources into cohesive, decision-oriented analytical stories with clear so what implications and actionable recommendations tailored to US brand and commercial audiences.
 Provide informal mentoring and quality guidance to Analyst I and Analyst II colleagues, reviewing their outputs, explaining methodological choices, and supporting their professional development.
 Contribute to team knowledge management by documenting analytical methods, data source guides, and reusable query libraries.
 KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & EXPERIENCE
 Education:
 Bachelor s Degree required in Statistics, Data Science, Life Sciences, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
 Master s degree or MBA in a relevant analytical or business field is strongly preferred.
 Experience:
 3-4 years of experience in US pharmaceutical commercial analytics or healthcare analytics, with demonstrated independent ownership of analytical deliverables.
 Deep working knowledge of US pharma commercial datasets: patient-level claims (IQVIA, Komodo Health, Symphony Health), specialty pharmacy (867), EMR, and sales data is required.
 Proficiency in coding techniques such as SQL, SAS, R, or Python for data manipulation and workflow automation; advanced Excel skills required.
 Strong experience with Tableau or Power BI for performance and brand analytics dashboard development.
 Exposure to Databricks or cloud-based analytics platforms is preferred.
 Experience supporting US brand teams or cross-functional analytics programs is strongly preferred.
 Skills & Competencies:
 Advanced US Commercial Data Analysis — Independently designs and executes complex analyses using multi-source US pharma commercial datasets; interprets results with strong domain context.
 Data Storytelling — Translates complex findings from multiple data sources into executive-ready narratives with explicit business implications and clear recommendations.
 Technical Proficiency — Advanced SQL; working Python/R; Tableau/Power BI; growing fluency in Databricks or cloud platforms.
 Stakeholder Communication — Communicates analytical findings persuasively to US I&A leads and cross-functional partners; adapts framing to both technical and commercial audiences.
 Automation and Efficiency — Proactively identifies and implements workflow improvements using code, pipelines, or process redesign.
 Informal Mentorship — Provides practical guidance to junior analysts on analytical techniques, data interpretation, and quality standards.
 Problem-Solving — Frames ambiguous business questions into structured hypotheses; comfortable navigating complexity and data limitations.
 TAKEDA LEADERSHIP BEHAVIORS
 In alignment with Takeda s Values-Based Culture, this role requires demonstration of the following leadership behaviors:
 Act with Integrity — Deliver accurate, transparent work and take full responsibility for quality.
 Collaborate Cross-Functionally — Contribute positively to GCC-US team workflows and cross-functional collaboration.
 Drive Accountability — Take ownership of all assigned tasks and deliver on commitments.
 Embrace Learning — Continuously build analytical and domain capabilities through feedback and self-driven development.
 BENEFITS: 
 It is our priority to provide competitive compensation and a benefit package that bridges your personal life with your professional career. Amongst our benefits are: 
 Competitive Salary Performance Annual Bonus 
 Flexible work environment, including hybrid working 
 Comprehensive Healthcare Insurance Plans for self, spouse, and children 
 Group Term Life Insurance and Group Accident Insurance programs 
 Employee Assistance Program 
 Broad Variety of learning platforms 
 Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Programs 
 Reimbursements – Home Internet & Mobile Phone 
 Employee Referral Program 
 Leaves – Paternity Leave (4 Weeks) , Maternity Leave (up to 26 weeks), Bereavement Leave (5 calendar days) 
 ABOUT ICC IN TAKEDA: 
 Takeda is leading a digital revolution. We’re not just transforming our company; we’re improving the lives of millions of patients who rely on our medicines every day. 
 As an organization, we are committed to our cloud-driven business transformation and believe the ICCs are the catalysts of change for our global organization. 
 #Li-Hybrid 
 
 
 Locations 
IND - Bengaluru 
 Worker Type 
Employee 
 Worker Sub-Type 
Regular 
 Time Type 
Full time

jobs.title: Project Financial Analyst II
jobs.description:
Project Financial Analyst II 
 Syneos Health® is a leading fully-integrated life sciences services organization built to accelerate customer success. We partner with innovators at every point across the drug development and commercialization continuum, helping them navigate complexity, anticipate change and accelerate progress.   Our Clinical Solutions team members act with a drug development mindset, applying their years of experience and deep expertise to truly understand customer needs and represent those in the solutions we shape.   Whether you join us in a Functional Service Provider partnership or a Full-Service environment, you’ll collaborate with passionate problem solvers, innovating as a team to help our customers achieve their goals. We are agile and driven to deliver – for one another, our customers, and, most importantly, for those in need.   Discover what your 25,000 future colleagues already know:   Why Syneos Health • We are passionate about developing our people, through career development and progression; supportive and engaged line management; technical and therapeutic area training; peer recognition and total rewards program. • We are committed to building an inclusive culture – where you can authentically be yourself. Central to this is our purpose – Driven to Deliver – which captures the passion of our colleagues to show up each day and shape solutions that have the ability to dramatically impact someone’s life.  • We are continuously building the company we all want to work for and our customers want to work with. Why? Because we know that when we bring together smart colleagues from across the world, we can shape the future of healthcare, driving impact for customers and defining the pace of patient progress. 
 
 Job Responsibilities 
 
 Core Responsibilities 
 Supports Business Unit Controller in:
 Records revenue each month for Unit, Fee for Service and Fixed Price contracts ;
 Loads project budgets into Oracle / Revenue Wizard and maintaining key member lists (project set up) and other project data in Oracle and associated systems and schedules;
 Drafts project level actual versus budget / forecast variance analysis monthly as well as monthly reports for distribution in analyzing monthly results;
 Drafts monthly forecasting of financial results ( backlog );
 Meets established deadlines for Revenue and Backlog submittals;
 Timely preparation of project close out reconciliations ;
 Reviews, with assistance, executed Work Orders and Change Orders for bonus or other anomalies.
 Works closely with Business Unit Controller to:
 Captures, assesses and consolidates results; and
 Drafts key reports such as annual cost and expense budgets, cost rates, cost and expense forecasting, monthly utilization, headcount, revenue backlog analysis, and/or gross profit for assigned area.
 Updates predefined reports.
 Attends project meetings as applicable to support assigned areas. Attends project kick-off meetings to capture and process relevant data for set up of newly awarded projects onto financial systems and schedules. Attends monthly project review (MPRs) meetings to ensure understanding/concurrence of project status, potential issues and the accuracy of the forecast .
 Monitors the overall health of assigned projects including but not limited to revenues, gross profit, receivables, and unbilled amounts as well as project close outs and reconciliations .
 
 Qualifications 
 Education Requirements 
 Minimum 4 Year / Bachelors Degree
 BA/BS degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, or
 equivalent education and experience.
 
 Additional Qualifications 
 Pharmaceutical or CRO experience preferred.
 Strong English proficiency. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) is essential.
 Proficiency with accounting software packages.
 Experience with Oracle Finance is preferred.
 Exceptional attention to detail, accuracy, organizational, interpersonal, and teamwork skills.
 Excellent financial, communication, and interpersonal skills as well as a high level of personal motivation to achieve.
 Demonstrated ability to work with confidential and sensitive data.
 Ability to perform several tasks simultaneously to meet deadlines is necessary.
 
 Critical Skills 
 Disclaimer 
 Tasks, duties, and responsibilities as listed in this job description are not exhaustive. The Company, at its sole discretion and with no prior notice, may assign other tasks, duties, and job responsibilities. Equivalent experience, skills, and/or education will also be considered so qualifications of incumbents
 may differ from those listed in the Job Description. The Company, at its sole discretion, will determine what constitutes as equivalent to the qualifications described above. Further, nothing contained herein should be construed to create an employment contract. Occasionally, required skills/experiences for jobs
 are expressed in brief terms. Any language contained herein is intended to fully comply with all obligations imposed by the legislation of each country in which it operates, including the implementation of the EU Equality Directive, in relation to the recruitment and employment of its employees. The Company is committed to compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, including the provision of reasonable accommodations, when appropriate, to assist employees or applicants to perform the essential functions of the job.
 
 US ONLY 
 Years of experience required 
 Minimum of zero to two (0 - 2) years of related experience
 
 Physical Requirements : Position requires: ability to occasionally move about inside the office to access filing cabinets, office equipment, etc. and to move between office locations; ability to maintain a stationaryposition for prolonged periods of time; ability to occasionally manipulate (e.g., lift, carry, move) office items and packages of light to medium weights of 10-35 pounds; ability to constantly operate a computer and other office productivity equipment (e.g., scanner, phone, printer, copier); ability to perceive and assess written materials for prolonged periods of time; and ability to frequently communicate with colleagues on the phone, by email, and in-person.
 
 Work Environment : This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, and filing cabinets. The noise level in the work environment is low. This is a largely sedentary role.
 
 
 At  Syneos Health, we believe in providing an environment and culture in which Our People can thrive, develop and advance. We reward and recognize our people by providing valuable benefits and a quality-of-life balance. The benefits for this position may include a company car or car allowance, Health benefits to include Medical, Dental and Vision, Company match 401k, eligibility to participate in Employee Stock Purchase Plan, Eligibility to earn commissions/bonus based on company and individual performance, and flexible paid time off (PTO) and sick time.  Because certain states and municipalities have regulated paid sick time requirements, eligibility for paid sick time may vary depending on where you work. Syneos complies with all applicable federal, state, and municipal paid sick time requirements.
 
 Salary Range:
$56,400.00 - $95,900.00 
 The base salary range represents the anticipated low and high of the Syneos Health range for this position. Actual salary will vary based on various factors such as the candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies, and proficiency for the role. 
 
 
 Get to know Syneos Health 
 Over the past 5 years, we have worked with 94% of all Novel FDA Approved Drugs, 95% of EMA Authorized Products and over 200 Studies across 73,000 Sites and 675,000 Trial patients.
 
 No matter what your role is, you’ll take the initiative and challenge the status quo with us in a highly competitive and ever-changing environment. Learn more about Syneos Health.
 
 http://www.syneoshealth.com

 Tasks, duties, and responsibilities as listed in this job description are not exhaustive.  The Company, at its sole discretion and with no prior notice, may assign other tasks, duties, and job responsibilities. jobs.title: Associate/Analyst – Commercial Pharma Analytics
jobs.description:
Job Title: Associate/Analyst – Commercial Pharma Analytics
 Career Level: C2/C3
 Introduction to role: 
 Are you ready to turn sophisticated brand data into decisions that help more patients receive the right medicines, as an Analyst in Brand Analytics, your role involves translating local customer signals and performance metrics into mentorship that advises brand strategy, resourcing, and customer engagement models.
 You will join a connected, digitally minded team that partners across markets and functions to raise the bar on how decisions get made. You will test new methods, introduce better tools, and create analytical practices that scale—so the enterprise can operate smarter today and be ready for tomorrow. Ready to be the voice of the customer in every strategic discussion?
 Accountabilities: 
 Customer Insight Leadership: Advocate for the application of local customer insight to inform brand strategy, prioritisation, and customer engagement models across markets.
 Analytic Capability Development: Determine and rank improvements and new analytic capabilities; lead the development, deployment, and embedding of these capabilities to elevate decision quality at pace.
 Advanced Methods and Technology: Continuously evaluate emerging quantitative methods, data assets, and technologies; bring in what works and retire what does not to keep the toolkit modern.
 Data Storytelling and Decision Influence: Convert raw data into significant conclusions and compelling narratives; present recommendations that build investment, targeting, and channel strategy.
 Data Access and Foundations: Access and integrate information from various tools and sources. Maintain a detailed understanding of critical data sets, governance, and modeling approaches to strengthen analytical foundations.
 Teamwork and Integration: Map and lead integration points with adjacent teams; align roadmaps and ensure analytics are embedded where decisions happen.
 Continuous Improvement: Instill a culture of testing, learning, and rapid deployment; measure impact and scale what delivers competitive advantage.
 Market and Competitive Scanning: Monitor external analytics trends within and beyond pharma; selectively embrace leading practices to sustain differentiation.
 Essential Skills/Experience: 
 The candidate will also be required to continuously evaluate new quantitative analysis methods and technologies.
 Develop a deep understanding of internal customers data and analytical needs focusing on the customer-facing model leadership.
 Foster the use of local customer knowledge in strategic and resourcing decisions across the business.
 Identify and prioritize improvements along with additional data analysis functions. Can coordinate the creation, rollout, and integration of such capabilities across the organization.
 Instills a culture of continuous improvement, testing, and deployment of new capabilities for the business.
 Develop innovative analytical practices to build and sustain competitive advantage.
 Access appropriate information through a variety of methods and channels
 Summarize the data in a much more data storytelling way and to present findings by redefining the raw metrics into actionable insights.
 Capability in applying deeper technical skills and knowledge that is shared across the functional area (e.g., deep knowledge of key data sets, modeling capabilities, etc.)
 Demonstrate a high level of understanding of the points of integration of our work and that of our colleagues.
 Monitors the external environment to stay up to date on leading analytic capabilities, both within and outside of pharma, which can be applied within the organization.
 Desirable Skills/Experience: 
 Experience in brand or commercial analytics within pharmaceuticals, healthcare, or another highly regulated industry.
 Proficiency with SQL and either Python or R; hands-on experience with visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
 Background in marketing science, including segmentation, targeting, MMM/MTA, CRM analytics, and next-best-action use cases.
 Shown experience operationalizing analytics into field force, omnichannel, or marketing platforms.
 Track record of working in agile settings dedicated to delivering analytics capabilities at scale.
 Strong communication and collaborator engagement skills, with the ability to influence senior decision-makers.
 Advanced degree in a quantitative field (e.g., statistics, economics, data science, engineering) is a plus.
 When we put unexpected teams in the same room, we unleash bold thinking with the power to inspire life-changing medicines. In-person working gives us the platform we need to connect, work at pace and challenge perceptions. That s why we work, on average, a minimum of three days per week from the office. But that doesn t mean we re not flexible. We balance the expectation of being in the office while respecting individual flexibility. Join us in our unique and ambitious world.
 Why AstraZeneca: 
 Here, your analysis does more than track performance—it accelerates how medicines reach people who need them. You will work shoulder to shoulder with diverse experts across the enterprise, using the latest digital tools and design-led approaches to improve how decisions get made. We bring unexpected teams together to test ideas quickly, take smart risks, and turn insight into action, while valuing kin
 
 
 Date Posted 
23-Jun-2026 
 Closing Date 
 
 
 AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity.  We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry-leading skills.  We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be.  We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics.  We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

jobs.title: Associate/Analyst – Social Media Listening
jobs.description:
Job Title: Associate/Analyst – Social Media Listening  
 Introduction to role:  
 Are you ready to turn real-time social conversation into decisions that accelerate our impact for patients and the business? As a Social Media Listening Associate/Analyst in Chennai, you will convert signals from global and local channels into clear, timely insights that guide content, media, and engagement decisions across our portfolio. Your analysis will help teams understand sentiment, surface emerging topics, and act quickly to improve how we communicate.
 You will join a fast paced analytics capability that partners closely with brand, communications, media, and digital teams. We move from question to answer in days, not months—designing queries, validating data, and delivering concise stories that change decisions. You will be trusted to run with complex datasets, speak up with recommendations, and see outcomes play out in live campaigns. Can you spot the signal in the noise and craft a story that teams can act on this week?
 Accountabilities:
 Topic and Taxonomy Design: Support the definition of topics, measures, and filters to frame clear objectives and deliverables for both standard and custom reports.
 Query Engineering and Tool Setup: Build and refine social listening queries, keywords, and parameters; configure dashboards to ensure accurate capture of volume, sentiment, drivers, and influencers.
 Data Quality and Refinement: Take ownership of query iteration to reduce noise and bias, ensuring precise, decision-ready mention analysis by the close of each reporting period.
 Insight Generation and Storytelling: Pull, interpret, and synthesize data into sharp insights that inform content and media decisions for campaigns and events; advise teams on optimizations and next actions.
 Reporting and Visualization: Create clear, visually compelling deliverables in PowerPoint, newsletters, and Excel—using charts, tables, and infographics to turn data into a coherent narrative.
 Campaign Optimization Partner: Translate findings into practical recommendations that impact targeting, creative, and channel mix; track outcomes and iterate to improve results.
 Standards and Compliance: Operate within global social listening SOPs and compliance policies; uphold data integrity and ethical standards at all times.
 Collaboration and Communication: Work closely with cross-functional partners to align on objectives, share findings succinctly, and influence decisions with evidence.  
 Essential Skills/Experience:
 2-4 years of experience
 Social Media Listening
 Bachelor’s Degree in any stream/Pharma or Mpharm (Pharmocology is preferred) or MBA
 Excellent written and verbal communication skills and proven ability to transform data into a concise, yet compelling story.
 Ability to synthesize information and produce insightful and accurate analysis and recommendations.
 Highly organized and impeccable attention to detail. Able to handle multiple projects simultaneously and deliver accurate reports without grammatical or data errors.
 Strong sense of responsibility, takes ownership, self-starter and proactive attitude.
 Good Mix of Technical Understanding, Business Sense, Managerial/Connecting and Interpersonal skills
 Strong familiarity and understanding of social media platforms (preferred) such as Facebook, Twitter, Blogs, Forums, Instagram and Tumblr required.
 Comfortable with MS Office Suite including Excel, PowerPoint.
 Exp in any Social Listening tools such as - Sprinklr, BrandWatch, Talkwalker, Meltwater, Netbase, Social Studio.
 Desirable Skills/Experience:
 Pharmaceutical and social listening experience is good to have
 Hands-on experience configuring queries, dashboards, and reports in enterprise social listening platforms
 Experience turning monitoring outputs into campaign or content optimizations within regulated environments
 Familiarity with global SOPs and compliance considerations for digital and social data
 Why AstraZeneca:
 Here, your curiosity and drive meet real-world impact. You will be part of a global, fast-evolving community that uses data to make better decisions—putting unexpected teams in the same room to unleash bold thinking and move quickly from insight to action. We value kindness alongside ambition, trust you to take ownership, and back you with a strong network that shares, learns, and builds together. Your analysis will help power decisions across the enterprise and, ultimately, contribute to outcomes that matter for patients and society.
 Call to Action:
 If you are ready to turn noise into insight and insight into action, step forward and show us how you’ll shape smarter decisions at speed.
 
 Date Posted 
26-Jun-2026 
 Closing Date 
12-Jul-2026 
 
 AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity.  We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry-leading skills.  We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be.  We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics.  We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

jobs.title: Analyst, Perception Analytics & Insights
jobs.description:
Working with Us Challenging. Meaningful. Life-changing. Those aren’t words that are usually associated with a job. But working at Bristol Myers Squibb is anything but usual. Here, uniquely interesting work happens every day, in every department. From optimizing a production line to the latest breakthroughs in cell therapy, this is work that transforms the lives of patients, and the careers of those who do it. You’ll get the chance to grow and thrive through opportunities uncommon in scale and scope, alongside high-achieving teams. Take your career farther than you thought possible.
 
 Bristol Myers Squibb recognizes the importance of balance and flexibility in our work environment. We offer a wide variety of competitive benefits, services and programs that provide our employees with the resources to pursue their goals, both at work and in their personal lives. Read more: careers.bms.com/working-with-us .
 
 
 Position Summary :
 The Analyst, Perception Analytics and Insights, supports the generation of data-driven insights by managing and curating internal and external datasets, including field medical insights and social media data. The role collaborates closely with team members to execute data operations, ranging from data acquisition and application of business rules to preparation of analysis-ready datasets and development of quantitative reports. Through this work, the analyst contributes to delivering structured insights while progressively building capabilities in analytics, data management, and innovative approaches under the guidance of senior team members.
 Roles & Responsibilities 
 Perform reporting and analysis on field medical insight and social media trends, patterns, and key reactions. Summarize findings and provide conclusions.
 Query, store, and manage internal insights and external social media data. Apply standardized business rules and automated processes to prepare analytic-ready data sets for a variety of purposes, including medical congress coverage and monitoring major product launches.
 Assist with devising and applying processes for the rapid and efficient handling of high volumes of unstructured text data, including leveraging innovative AI and algorithmic techniques to provide accurate categorization and tagging for business purposes.
 Appropriately maintain tools and processes used in the consistent processing of data, including product/study tag libraries, master user mapping references, and other files. Create efficient pipelines for the curation of analytic-ready data sets.
 Maintain and populate PowerPoint templates for quantitative insight reporting for a variety of purposes, including congresses, Medical launches, and ad hoc analytic needs. Assist with the creation of other reports, including social media reporting, based on direction from managers and onshore leads.
 Stay informed about industry trends relevant to BI reporting, visualization, medical affairs, and Field Medical.
 Skills and competencies 
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
 Analytic mindset with knowledge of qualitative design, data analysis, and applied statistics.
 Excellent organizational skills and ability to work on multiple priorities simultaneously.
 Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines.
 Ability to work independently and as part of a cross-functional team.
 Good written and verbal communication skills, both when working on reports and communicating with stakeholders.
 Experience 
 BA/BS minimum degree. Special consideration for advanced degrees in applied sciences (computer science, mathematics, data science) or engineering.
 1-3 years career experience preferred, including experience in data analytics.
 Ability to work collaboratively with colleagues and stakeholders in a challenging and fast-paced matrix environment. Ability to understand business goals and priorities, translating them into actionable reporting.
 Good written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to summarize complex information into easy-to-digest written formats.
 Clinical or pharmaceutical / healthcare industry experience preferred.
 Familiarity with how data can be used to answer critical business solutions and develop creative solutions.
 Familiarity with different analytics and data visualization methodologies, with an emphasis on text analytics. Experience with integrating new tools and methodologies into complex data environments would be a bonus.
 Familiarity with sourcing social media data (Twitter/X) from external sources.
 Proficiency with MS Office
 Proficiency in interactive and collaborative workspaces (e.g. SharePoint, Domino)
 Familiarity with predictive analytics software and methodologies (e.g. R, Python, SAS)
 Familiarity with text analytics software and methodologies (e.g. BioBERT, SciBERT, Luminoso, KANA)
 Familiarity with data visualization software (Tableau)
 Pharmaceutical industry experience preferred
 
 If you come across a role that intrigues you but doesn’t perfectly line up with your resume, we encourage you to apply anyway. You could be one step away from work that will transform your life and career. 
 
 Uniquely Interesting Work, Life-changing Careers With a single vision as inspiring as “Transforming patients’ lives through science™ ”, every BMS employee plays an integral role in work that goes far beyond ordinary. Each of us is empowered to apply our individual talents and unique perspectives in a supportive culture, promoting global participation in clinical trials, while our shared values of passion, innovation, urgency, accountability, inclusion and integrity bring out the highest potential of each of our colleagues.
 
 On-site Protocol 
 BMS has an occupancy structure that determines where an employee is required to conduct their work. This structure includes site-essential, site-by-design, field-based and remote-by-design jobs. The occupancy type that you are assigned is determined by the nature and responsibilities of your role: 
 
 Site-essential roles require 100% of shifts onsite at your assigned facility. Site-by-design roles may be eligible for a hybrid work model with at least 50% onsite at your assigned facility. For these roles, onsite presence is considered an essential job function and is critical to collaboration, innovation, productivity, and a positive Company culture. For field-based and remote-by-design roles the ability to physically travel to visit customers, patients or business partners and to attend meetings on behalf of BMS as directed is an essential job function. 
 
 Supporting People with Disabilities 
 BMS is dedicated to ensuring that people with disabilities can excel through a transparent recruitment process, reasonable workplace accommodations/adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Applicants can request a reasonable workplace accommodation/adjustment prior to accepting a job offer. If you require reasonable accommodations/adjustments in completing this application, or in any part of the recruitment process, direct your inquiries to adastaffingsupport bms.com . Visit careers.bms.com/ eeo -accessibility to access our complete Equal Employment Opportunity statement. 
 
 Candidate Rights 
 BMS will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, pursuant to applicable laws in your area. 
 
 If you live in or expect to work from Los Angeles County if hired for this position, please visit this page for important additional information:  https://careers.bms.com/california-residents/ 
 
 Data Protection 
 We will never request payments, financial information, or social security numbers during our application or recruitment process. Learn more about protecting yourself at https://careers.bms.com/fraud-protection .
 
 Any data processed in connection with role applications will be treated in accordance with applicable data privacy policies and regulations.
 
 If you believe that the job posting is missing information required by local law or incorrect in any way, please contact BMS at TAEnablement bms.com . Please provide the Job Title and Requisition number so we can review. Communications related to your application should not be sent to this email and you will not receive a response. Inquiries related to the status of your application should be directed to Chat with Ripley. 
 
 
R1603645 : Analyst, Perception Analytics & Insights

jobs.title: Tax Analyst
jobs.description:
GROWING WHAT MATTERS STARTS WITH YOU 
 
 As the world’s only major agriscience company completely dedicated to agriculture, we’re building a culture that stays curious, thinks differently, acts boldly, and takes a stand on what’s right for our customers, our co-workers, our partners and our planet. We know we’ve got big challenges to solve - we hope you ll be part of the solution. Working at Corteva connects you with more than 20,000 colleagues united by a shared vision to grow what matters. We offer career opportunities across more than 140 world-class R&D facilities and in more than 130 countries. 
 
 We’re hiring for Tax Analyst to join our Finance team!
 
 Learn how you can be our voice in the conversation about the future of agriculture.
 
 You Will Be Part of Growing Team 
 
 Finance is a Global team tasked with supporting Finance processing requests across various regions.
 
 Our team is comprised of members supporting and providing support to the Finance Team and business across from various regions. 
 
 The role will be performed within the frame of Corteva’ s Brand values: 
 
 Job responsibilities 
 
 Senior Tax Specialist position in CGSC US Tax process is the role to prepare & support various US Direct tax compliance activities.
 Prepare U.S. corporate income tax returns (Form 1120) for parent and subsidiary entities, ensuring accuracy and compliance with IRS regulations.
 Perform quarterly tax provision calculations, including book-to-tax adjustments and accruals for multiple legal entities.
 Prepare international tax forms including Forms 5471 (Controlled Foreign Corporations), 8858 (Foreign disregarded entities), and 8865 (Controlled Foreign Partnerships) for global operations.
 Prepare Earnings & Profits (E&P) workpapers for international entities to support tax compliance and reporting requirements.
 Prepare and file federal income tax extensions for corporations and partnerships within prescribed timelines.
 Utilize OneSource Income Tax software for tax return preparation, data management, and compliance processes.
 
  Business & Teamwork 
 Working with team and clients to ensure delivery as per agreed timelines.
 Publishing performance dashboards and required tax specific reporting.
 Ensure proactive controls in place to highlight issues before they are picked up by business.
 Develop an in-depth knowledge of accounting flows tax framework to support the process.
 
 Principal Accountabilities: Key activities 
 Ensure 100% service delivery and adherence to all Key Control Activities.
 Ensure the basic process documentation is completed for all the activities.
 
 Location: 
 
 Corteva Global Service Center, Hyderabad, India
 
 To Grow What Matters ,You Will Need: 
 Graduates with US Tax background with 2 – 5 years of experience.
 Ability to develop effective working relationships with business stakeholders.
 An excellent attention to detail.
 Very good working knowledge of Microsoft Office is essential.
 Position requires strong analytical skills, ability to multi-task, and proficiency in the use of tax software like OneSource.
 Ability to research and understand principles of U.S. tax law.
 Should have demonstrated the ability to drive results.
 
 Who Are We Looking For? 
 Curious, bold thinkers who want to grow their careers and be part of a winning team.
 Market shaping individuals who want to transform the agriculture industry to meet the world’s growing need for food.
 Collaborators who thrive in a diverse, inclusive work environment
 Innovators who bring initiative and fresh ideas that drive our business into the future and make us an industry leader.
 
 GROWING WHAT MATTERS STARTS WITH YOU… WHAT CAN WE OFFER TO HELP YOU GROW? 
 Opportunity to be part of a global industry leader working to discover solutions to the most pressing agricultural challenges of our time.
 Challenging work assignments that grow your skills, capabilities and experiences.
 Diverse, inclusive work environment where employees bring their whole selves to work and feel heard, valued and empowered.
 Dedicated and customized resources to help grow your professional skills, industry expertise and personal perspectives.
 Opportunity to strengthen your professional network through valuable relationships.
 Support for the health and well-being of every employee by offering world-class benefits, meaningful work and competitive salary.
 Performance driven culture with a strong focus on speed, accountability and agility.

jobs.title: Business Process Analyst
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Business Process Analyst to join our Analytics & Digital Solutions (ADS) organization. In this role, you will lead initiatives that deliver scalable, action‑oriented analytics solutions supporting Global Logistics function.
 You will partner closely with Logistics, Operations Business Leaders (OBLs) and data engineering teams to co‑create high‑quality analytics products, support design reviews, and enable structured deployments. As a functional analyst, you will also drive adoption, change management, and sustained utilization of logistics analytics solutions. This role supports global and regional logistics operations by delivering data‑driven insights and analytics products that improve process efficiency, service performance, cost optimization, and continuous improvement , with solutions designed to scale across regions where applicable.
 What’s in it For You •    The opportunity to take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments •    The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth •    The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best •    This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly recognized global growth company offering competitive compensation and benefits in addition to career growth
 Key Responsibilities 
 Partner with Logistics leadership and regional teams to translate operational needs into analytics solutions
 Collaborate with global and regional leaders to standardize and scale logistics analytics solutions 
 Act as a business process expert and contribute to knowledge management, documentation, and continuous improvement  
 Stay current on industry and analytics trends to identify opportunities for new technologies and practices
 Act as the functional SME for logistics reporting and analytics tools, enabling faster insights and improved effectiveness
 Support process efficiency, automation, and controls initiatives across logistics operations to improve operations.
 Co‑develop AI‑ready analytical data models with data engineering and operations teams to enable action‑oriented logistics insights
 Design and deliver reporting for logistics KPIs , including transportation, service, cost, and execution performance
 Ensure data availability, reliability, quality, validation, and reconciliation across all logistics analytics products
 Lead design reviews, functional QA, and governance for dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions
 Identify logistics and transportation process, service and cost improvement opportunities , including AI‑enabled use cases
 Ideate, prototype, and iterate solutions using analytical tools such as Power BI , python etc. balancing analytical rigor with usability
 Plan and support analytics deployments , including documentation and delivery readiness
 
 Minimum Qualifications 
 Bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, information systems, supply chain , or a related field
 E xperience in analytics, logistics, transportation, supply chain, or business process roles
 Good SAP experience in Logistics function.
 Excellent analytical and problem‑solving skills with strong attention to detail
 Proficiency in Power BI and working knowledge of SQL, Python, Azure and Snowflake. 
 Strong communication, organization, and collaboration skills
 Proven ability to drive change management and value realization through analytics solutions
 Ability to manage multiple initiatives in a fast‑paced environment
 Experience working in Warehouse Management System (WMS) and Transportation Management System (TMS) 
 Working knowledge of ETL processes and data warehousing 
 Preferred Qualifications 
 Experience working with SAP logistics, transportation and warehousing. 
 Experience working with Transportation management systems such as Blue Yonder, Mercury Gate etc will be added advantage . 
 Experience working with Warehouse management systems such as Körber etc will be added advantage . 
 Advanced degree or certification in supply chain, logistics, analytics, or computer science such as ASCM CLTD etc.

jobs.title: Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, delivering critical insights and innovative solutions to help our customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments. With our worldwide reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to own your future and impact what matters. Are you ready to make an impact?
 
 Ecolab Company has an opening for Financial Planning Analyst.  If you are a passionate professional that is seeking opportunity, advancement, and a rewarding career we invite you to apply. This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly recognized global growth company offering competitive compensation and benefits in addition to career growth.
 The Financial Planning & Analysis Analyst will be responsible for financial planning and analysis activities supporting business divisions. This position will require partnership across multiple stakeholders within the organization and will have significant exposure and growth opportunities supported by a management team committed to associate development.
 
 What You Will Do: 
 Analyze trends for Ecolab divisions, including volume and pricing and provide insights to the divisional teams on Key performance metrics
 Coordinate monthly close and forecast activities, including communicating guidance, facilitating system loads, analyzing results Sales, Gross Profit & SG&A), and preparing content for financial reviews with senior leadership
 Perform Operation income reconciliation from Forecast to Actuals
 Prepare Daily Sales Report and project the month to go estimates Vs forecast
 Primary owner of developing a driver-based 50/50 forecast
 Partner with division finance teams to understand external and internal business dynamics and their impact on results
 Lead the creation of annual plan by leveraging the trend based analytical model to arrive at a high-level proposal & coordinate/communicate with multiple key stakeholders
 Develop sustainable content for use in monthly business reviews (MBRs) and provide ad-hoc financial analytics and deep dives to facilitate decision making
 Partner with broader stakeholders to drive critical reporting needs and capabilities
 
 Qualifications: 
 Chartered Accountant /MBA premier business school
 5 plus years of experience in financial Planning & Analysis in FMCG / Manufacturing industry
 Ability to translate financial analysis into insights and action plans
 Strong analytical, critical thinking and problem-solving skills
 Strong verbal and written communication skills and the ability to interact with senior management
 information with a variety of audiences, including those with limited finance background
 Self-motivated with ability to efficiently organize and prioritize competing priorities
 Demonstrated attention to detail and ability to develop, implement and interpret financial models
 Strong PC and systems skills (Power BI, Microsoft Excel, and PowerPoint) 
 
 
 A trusted partner for millions of customers, Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene and infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and the resources vital to life. Building on a century of innovation, Ecolab has annual sales of $15 billion, employs more than 47,000 associates and operates in more than 170 countries around the world. The company delivers comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights and world-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, and optimize water and energy use. Ecolab’s innovative solutions improve operational efficiencies and sustainability for customers in the food, healthcare, life sciences, hospitality and industrial markets. www.ecolab.com
 
 
 Follow us on LinkedIn Ecolab, Twitter Ecolab, Instagram Ecolab_Inc and Facebook Ecolab.

jobs.title: Product Development Analyst
jobs.description:
At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, delivering critical insights and innovative solutions to help our customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments. With our worldwide reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to own your future and impact what matters. Are you ready to make an impact?
 
 Ecolab is seeking a BI Analyst to join the Global Business Intelligence (BI) team, contributing to BI development efforts and driving business process improvement through analytics and reporting. This role requires proven proficiency in the Microsoft BI stack and familiarity with Snowflake and cloud-based data architectures. The ideal candidate will work on complex, cross-functional projects and collaborate with technical and business stakeholders across finance and sales teams.
 
 What’s in it For You: 
 You will join a growth company offering a competitive salary and benefits. 
 The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
 The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.
 Feel proud each day to work for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments.
 
 What You Will Do: 
 Design and develop data visualizations and dashboards that communicate insights and support decision-making.
 Contribute to BI dashboard development on cross-functional projects; manage various phases of project lifecycles.
 Define and adhere to enterprise BI development standards and best practices.
 Engage with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into reporting solutions.
 Work with data engineers to support data pipelines and ensure sustainability and performance of reporting solutions.
 Optimize dashboard performance and user experience to maximize adoption and impact.
 Contribute to deployment strategies and project-specific delivery plans.
 Promote data-driven decision-making and the adoption of self-service BI tools across divisions and markets.
 Provide input on data governance, reporting strategy, and business insights.
 Participate in wireframe and dashboard design discussions.
 
 Technical Environment 
 Microsoft Power BI and Azure Analysis Services (AAS)
 Snowflake (Cloud Data Warehouse)
 SQL/T-SQL, stored procedures, data modeling
 Data warehousing, ETL, and reporting architecture
 
 Minimum Qualifications: 
 Bachelor’s degree in computer science, MIS, Statistics, Business, or a related quantitative field.
 5 years of experience developing data visualizations and self-service BI dashboards.
 3 years of SQL programming and experience with data extraction from complex datasets.
 Proficiency with Power BI, including data modeling, DAX, and dashboard design.
 Strong report and visualization design skills aligned with end-user needs.
 Strong data analytical, validation and troubleshooting skills of systems and reports
 Ability to transform complex data into actionable insights.
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 Understand business questions related to analytics, with the ability to communicate data insights through visually compelling reports.
 Experience interacting with a diverse, global team across multiple business segments; comfortable working with various organizational levels.
 Basic understanding of financial and accounting principles.
 Excellent communication and documentation skills.
 Strong organizational skills, able to successfully manage core responsibilities and balance multiple priorities.
 Proven experience translating business requirements into scalable BI artifacts (reports, dashboards, KPIs).
 Experience with BI architecture, data modeling, and ETL development.
 Experience with other data analytics tools such as Python or R.
 Experience in using support tools such as ServiceNow and ADO.
 
 
 A trusted partner for millions of customers, Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene and infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and the resources vital to life. Building on a century of innovation, Ecolab has annual sales of $14 billion, employs more than 47,000 associates and operates in more than 170 countries around the world. The company delivers comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights and world-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, and optimize water and energy use. Ecolab’s innovative solutions improve operational efficiencies and sustainability for customers in the food, healthcare, life sciences, hospitality and industrial markets. www.ecolab.com
 
 Follow us on LinkedIn Ecolab, Twitter Ecolab, Instagram Ecolab_Inc and Facebook Ecolab.
