jobs.description:
At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, delivering critical insights and innovative solutions to help our customers achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments. With our worldwide reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to own your future and impact what matters. Are you ready to make an impact?
 
 Ecolab is seeking a Product Development Analyst to join the Global Business Intelligence (BI) team, contributing to BI development efforts and driving business process improvement through analytics and reporting. This role requires proven proficiency in the Microsoft BI stack and familiarity with Snowflake and cloud-based data architectures. The ideal candidate will work on complex, cross-functional projects and collaborate with technical and business stakeholders across finance and sales teams.
 
 What’s in it For You: 
 You will join a growth company offering a competitive salary and benefits. 
 The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
 The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.
 Feel proud each day to work for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments.
 
 What You Will Do: 
 Design and develop data visualizations and dashboards that communicate insights and support decision-making.
 Contribute to BI dashboard development on cross-functional projects; manage various phases of project lifecycles.
 Define and adhere to enterprise BI development standards and best practices.
 Engage with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into reporting solutions.
 Work with data engineers to support data pipelines and ensure sustainability and performance of reporting solutions.
 Optimize dashboard performance and user experience to maximize adoption and impact.
 Contribute to deployment strategies and project-specific delivery plans.
 Promote data-driven decision-making and the adoption of self-service BI tools across divisions and markets.
 Provide input on data governance, reporting strategy, and business insights.
 Participate in wireframe and dashboard design discussions.
 
 Technical Environment 
 Microsoft Power BI and Azure Analysis Services (AAS)
 Snowflake (Cloud Data Warehouse)
 SQL/T-SQL, stored procedures, data modeling
 Data warehousing, ETL, and reporting architecture
 
 Minimum Qualifications: 
 Bachelor’s degree in computer science, MIS, Statistics, Business, or a related quantitative field.
 5 years of experience developing data visualizations and self-service BI dashboards.
 3 years of SQL programming and experience with data extraction from complex datasets.
 Proficiency with Power BI, including data modeling, DAX, and dashboard design.
 Strong report and visualization design skills aligned with end-user needs.
 Strong data analytical, validation and troubleshooting skills of systems and reports
 Ability to transform complex data into actionable insights.
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 Understand business questions related to analytics, with the ability to communicate data insights through visually compelling reports.
 Experience interacting with a diverse, global team across multiple business segments; comfortable working with various organizational levels.
 Basic understanding of financial and accounting principles.
 Excellent communication and documentation skills.
 Strong organizational skills, able to successfully manage core responsibilities and balance multiple priorities.
 Proven experience translating business requirements into scalable BI artifacts (reports, dashboards, KPIs).
 Experience with BI architecture, data modeling, and ETL development.
 Experience with other data analytics tools such as Python or R.
 Experience in using support tools such as ServiceNow and ADO.
 
 A trusted partner for millions of customers, Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene and infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and the resources vital to life. Building on a century of innovation, Ecolab has annual sales of $14 billion, employs more than 47,000 associates and operates in more than 170 countries around the world. The company delivers comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights and world-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, and optimize water and energy use. Ecolab’s innovative solutions improve operational efficiencies and sustainability for customers in the food, healthcare, life sciences, hospitality and industrial markets. www.ecolab.com
 
 Follow us on LinkedIn Ecolab, Twitter Ecolab, Instagram Ecolab_Inc and Facebook Ecolab.

jobs.title: ServiceNow Business Process Analyst/QA
jobs.description:
ServiceNow Business Process Analyst/QA -   CSM, FSM, MCO, APO Focused 
 
 Key Responsibilities 
 Job Summary:  In this role you will partner with individuals across the organization, at various levels, including divisional business teams, product owners, IT teams and ServiceNow team members (Service Delivery and Operations teams) to facilitate user story gathering, Testing  and design sessions, and collaboratively define solutions that address key opportunities
 
 Key Responsibilities: 
 Requirements gathering, business analysis implementation, support and services of Development cycle (Agile Methodology, SAFe, Waterfall).
 Build Service now ATF’s and test cases and SOPs
 Mention ability to design regression test suites for upgrades and new releases.
 Show how business requirements are translated into functional test cases.
 Emphasize traceability: requirements → test cases → validation results.
 Position yourself as the bridge ensuring test cases align with business needs.
 Responsible for project goals and objectives, key process decisions and alignment with project stakeholders related to large projects, KPIs projections, tracking, and reporting for assigned initiatives
 Translate business needs into functional specifications and process flows
 Identify gaps, inefficiencies, and opportunities for automation and optimization
 Define and Owns HLD and LLD from functional standpoint however shall not be limited to only functional aspects alone and may extend further to business knowledge also.
 Conduct research and analysis to support business decision-making.
 Collaborate with stakeholders to identify and define project requirements.
 Support the development of project plans and timelines, Upgrade activities.
 Assist in the preparation of reports and presentations for management.
 Participate in meetings and provide input on business processes and improvements.
 Perform other duties as assigned by the Senior Business Analyst or Manager like defining SOP’s , Knowledge Articles, Training and Scope or other necessary artifacts.
 Recommend continuous improvements leveraging ServiceNow’s evolving capabilities.
 
 Minimum Qualifications: 
 Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Information Technology, or a related field.
 4 years of experience in business analysis or related role.
 Good Understanding  with ServiceNow configuration, Basics scripting , and integration capabilities.
 Strong functional experience in ITSM, CMDB, Workflow/Flow designer, Service Portals/ESC , Virtual Agentic AI, Predictive Intelligence, Workspace, Process Automation playbooks
 Good Understanding on ServiceNow foundational data and data models for more than 2 applications (ITSM, CSM, HRSD, SPM)
 Good Understating of ServiceNow best practices.
 ITSM and CMDB experience is must have.
 CSM, ITOM, FSM experience is a plus
 Hands on experience in Reporting, PA and ATF or other testing tools is plus.
 
 Skills: 
 Good in building functional, technical specs, Presentation based on project scope, Process Flows based on Business processes
 Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities.
 Good knowledge of data analysis and reporting.
 Familiarity with project management software is a plus.
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
 Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
 Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Visio, Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
 Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
 High understanding of business analysis methodologies and tools.
 
 Nice to Have  
 Certification ITIL Expert or   ITIL v3/v4 
 Certifications on ServiceNow CSA and Business Analyst from other institutions 
 Experience supporting global enterprise environments.

jobs.title: Analyst - Global Customer Request Team LS-I
jobs.description:
Ashland India Private Ltd 
 
 Position:  Analyst - Global Regulatory Request Team LS-I 
 
 Job Description 
 
 Are you detail-oriented, customer-focused, and passionate about regulatory compliance? Do you enjoy solving complex customer inquiries and collaborating with global teams to deliver timely and accurate responses? Are you driven by continuous learning and effective communication in a fast-paced environment?
 
 If you thrive on managing customer requests, navigating regulatory requirements, and working across regions to support business needs, we would like to meet you.
 
 The Analyst - Global Regulatory Request Team LS-I :  is responsible for:
 
 Coordinate customer requests related to product regulatory inquiries.
 Complete customer questionnaires and coordinate responses with regional and global Product Regulatory and Quality team members.
 Work closely with internal and external customers to communicate the status of requests and ensure timely resolution.
 Serve as the first point of contact for customer questionnaires and provide accurate and appropriate responses.
 Take ownership of assigned requests, proactively following up on each case to ensure completion in a timely and high-quality manner.
 Develop an understanding of customer regulatory requirements in both industrial and consumer product environments.
 Maintain accurate records of customer requests and support continuous process improvement initiatives.
 Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure regulatory information is current, consistent, and compliant with customer requirements.
 Support global business objectives by delivering excellent customer service and regulatory guidance.
 
 Education / Experience 
 
 Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent in Chemistry, Physical Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, or a related technical discipline.
 Minimum 2 years of experience in Product Regulatory functions within the chemical industry.
 Experience with pharmaceutical-grade products and knowledge of solvent-based chemistry is preferred.
 Proficient in speaking, reading, and writing English. Additional Asian or European language skills are an advantage.
 Ability to work with global teams and support activities across European and U.S. business hours as required.
 
 Key Interactions:             
 Internal: Internal stakeholders, Ashland Teams
 External: Customers, suppliers, vendors
 
 
 In more than 100 countries, the people of Ashland LLC. (NYSE: ASH) provide the specialty chemicals, technologies and insights to help customers create new and improved products for today and sustainable solutions for tomorrow. Our chemistry is at work every day in a wide variety of markets and applications, including architectural coatings, automotive, construction, energy, personal care and pharmaceutical. Visit www.ashland.com to see the innovations we offer. At Ashland our vision is to be a leading, global specialty chemicals company whose inspired and engaged employees add value to all we touch. In fact our people, employees, customers and vendors define who we are. They are the driving force behind everything we do. Not only do we value our customers but we value our employees, and we work to offer them a dynamic and challenging environment. We hold ourselves to high standards at Ashland, and we value integrity and honesty. Ashland has a history of attracting the best people and keeping them. The reasons are simple: industry competitive salary and benefits, pay-for-performance incentive plans and a diverse work environment where employees feel challenged and valued. People come to Ashland and stay. As a growing Fortune 500 specialty chemicals company, we offer opportunities for development and advancement throughout our global organization. Our values define who we are and what we care about as a company. If you are looking for a relationship with a company instead of simply a job, this may be a great fit. 
 Ashland is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer Minorities/Women/Veterans/Disabled/Gender Identity/Sexual Orientation. 
 
 All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. NOTE: We do not accept resumes from external staffing agencies or independent recruiters for any of our openings unless we have a signed recruiting agreement in place to fill a specific position.

jobs.title: Orchestration Analyst
jobs.description:
As the Orchestration Analyst , you will play a crucial role in driving data-driven decision-making processes and optimizing business performance in the Global Business Services (GBS) space. Your responsibilities will include developing and maintaining global reports, performing data quality checks, and supporting ad-hoc analyses. You should have a strong technical background in data visualization, SQL, and tools such as Alteryx. You will be responsible for gathering, analyzing, and visualizing data from various sources to provide valuable insights and support strategic initiatives across the organization. Additionally, you will focus on improving and automating analytic processes to enhance efficiency and accuracy.
 
 Location: 
 Pune
 
 What’s in it for you: 
 The opportunity to take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges, helping customers achieve clean water, safe food, and healthy environments
 The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
 The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
 Be part of an outstanding team with relentless focus on improvement and exceeding our customers’ expectations
 
 What You Will Do: 
 Data Management and Analysis:
 Utilize Power BI and ThoughtSpot to develop interactive and visually compelling reports and dashboards that enable stakeholders to make informed decisions
 Perform data mining and modeling tasks using SQL and Python, uncovering hidden opportunities and providing insights into business performance
 Conduct data quality checks and support day-to-day analytic needs
 Continuously seek opportunities to enhance data analysis processes, tools, and methodologies
 
 Communication and Collaboration:
 Communicate complex data findings to non-technical audiences through intuitive visualizations
 Collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive data-driven decision-making
 
 Process Improvement and Automation:
 Implement and manage analytic process improvements and automation using tools such as Alteryx
 Stay updated with the latest trends and advancements in data analytics, business intelligence, and data visualization tools
 
 Minimum Qualifications: 
 Bachelor s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Finance, or a related field
 Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role
 Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a keen attention to detail
 Strong communication skills to effectively convey insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders across multiple functions
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 Proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools such as Power BI and ThoughtSpot
 Proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write queries, transform, blend, and manipulate data from databases
 Experience with Alteryx, or similar tools, for analytic process improvement and automation is preferred
 Ability to work independently and collaborate effectively in a team-oriented environment
 
 Tools: 
 Power BI/ThoughtSpot, basic SQL, Alteryx

jobs.title: Business Intelligence/Data Analytics Specialist
jobs.description:
BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE/DATA ANALYTICS SPECIALIST     
 LOCATION: Hyderabad  
 
 INTRO SUMMARY OF THE ROLE    
 At Lonza, we are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our Group Intelligence team. This role supports the technical delivery and day-to-day operation of Lonza’s Business Intelligence platforms, with a primary focus on Commercial, Sales, and Marketing use cases. You will contribute across the full BI lifecycle—from data ingestion and modelling through to analytics, applications, and user-facing reporting—ensuring BI products are reliable, scalable, and aligned to business needs. 
 
 WHAT YOU WILL GET    
 At Lonza, we offer a collaborative environment guided by our core values: Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion, and Integrity. You will benefit from professional growth opportunities, exposure to global teams, and the chance to shape BI solutions that directly impact business performance. (Region-specific benefits such as healthcare, retirement plans, and relocation support should be added here per the regional template.) 
 
 WHAT YOU WILL DO 
 Act as an active member of the Omni BI project team, supporting delivery and evolution of BI products. 
 Gather business requirements and scope solutions with a strong focus on end users. 
 Develop ETL processes, manage data integration and pipelines. 
 Design and deliver user-centric reports and dashboards with strong storytelling and usability principles. 
 Generate analytics and insights to support commercial decision-making. 
 Strengthen data foundations by improving data quality, availability, and traceability. 
 Provide operational support for BI platforms, including monitoring, issue resolution, and performance optimization. 
 Contribute to data governance through standards, metadata management, and access controls. 
 Identify opportunities for BI, analytics, or automation to deliver additional value, primarily within Marketing. 
 Encourage innovation through proof-of-concept development and continuous improvement of tools and processes. 
 
 WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR 
 Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data, IT, Digital, or related fields. 
 Experience: Entry-level (0–4 years) in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or related areas. 
 Technical Skills:
 Strong BI and data skills, with experience in database-driven environments. 
 Hands-on ETL experience with tools such as Snaplogic, SSIS, Azure Data Factory. 
 Awareness of data governance concepts (quality, lineage, controlled access). 
 Experience designing reports/dashboards using Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Spotfire, Looker, or equivalent. 
 Advantageous: Experience with JavaScript frameworks (React, Angular, Vue, Node.js), containerized environments (Docker), CI/CD practices. 
 
 
 About Lonza At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30 sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. 
 Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. 
 Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

jobs.title: SAR Cost Controller & Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Title: SAR Cost Controller & Analyst 
 Location: Gurugram, India 
 
 We are looking for a hands-on SAR Cost Controller & Analyst to strengthen financial controlling and business partnering within the FP&A organization. In this role, you will drive cost transparency, ensure accurate financial reporting, and support decision-making through insightful analysis.
 Working closely with Global Function Leads and Finance stakeholders, you will play a key role in planning, forecasting, and cost optimization, while continuously improving financial processes and leveraging digital tools to enhance efficiency and data quality.
 
 What you will get: 
 Opportunity to act as a trusted Finance Business Partner, influencing decisions across global functions
 Exposure to end-to-end financial cycles including planning, budgeting, forecasting, and reporting
 Ownership of cost controlling and analysis for cost centers, projects, and FTEs
 Involvement in digitalization and automation initiatives within finance
 Collaborative environment with cross-functional teams including R&D and global stakeholders
 A role that combines operational execution with strategic financial insights
 
 What you will do: 
 Financial Business Partnering & Analysis 
 Act as a hands-on Finance Business Partner to Global Function Lead, and R&D department
 Analyze cost drivers at cost center and departmental level and clearly explain variances
 Provide financial guidance on costs, consulting spend, FTEs, and projects
 Identify cost risks and optimization opportunities, recommending corrective actions
 Support budget, forecast (Outlook), and long-range planning cycles
 Prepare financial analysis and insights to support business decision-making
 Cost Control & Financial Operations 
 Execute month-end closing activities (accruals, reclassifications, allocations, FTE postings)
 Monitor actuals vs. budget/forecast, investigate variances, and provide commentary
 Manage cost center and FTE planning processes
 Support R&D controlling, business cases, and capitalization activities
 Track project budgets and spending, ensuring financial compliance
 Ensure data quality, integrity, and timeliness in reporting
 Support audits, projects, and business changes (e.g., M&A activities)
 Digitalization & Process Improvement 
 Drive automation and digitalization of reporting and controlling processes
 Identify and implement process improvements and efficiencies
 Promote standardization and best practices across finance operations
 Leverage data analytics and AI-enabled tools to enhance insights and detect anomalies
 Support change initiatives and adoption of new tools and ways of working
 
 What we are looking for: 
 Strong experience ~5–7 years of  experience in finance or controlling in financial controlling, cost analysis, and business partnering
 Solid understanding of planning, budgeting, and forecasting processes
 Ability to translate financial data into actionable insights for non-finance stakeholders
 Experience working with cost centers, project controlling, and financial reporting
 Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving and attention to detail
 Proven ability to collaborate across functions in a global environment
 Hands-on approach with ownership and accountability
 
 At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30 sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
 
 Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
 
 Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

jobs.title: Business Process Analyst
jobs.description:
RESPONSIBILITIES: 
 Collaborate with business analysts and other business-facing partners to identify and define technical and data requirements.
 Translate requirements into high-level technical designs that serve as inputs into solution development.
 Lead the configuration, setup, testing, training, and implementations.
 Collaborate with the development team to communicate business and technical requirements
 Provide implementation guidance.
 Partner with the development team to brainstorm, analyze and recommend solutions that provide new features and functionality.
 Participate in all aspects of the agile/scrum development process.
 Support test and production deployments to ensure delivery of a high-quality product.
 Demonstrate ability to effectively communicate with representatives, business partners, and other stakeholders
 Involve daily in complex business problem resolution requiring higher-level critical thinking.
 Own Strategic Analysis Responsibilities
 Closely work with the business/product and architects and drive design decisions
 Drive various data-driven decision (KPIs, User feedback, BI reports, etc.) on the product
 Drive the team effort to effectively solve business problems
 In-depth analysis of all business requirements and derive appropriate user and data flows, represented with flow charts and diagrams along with effective stories written in requirement management tool (Azure DevOps)
 Recommend enhancements in systems to improve efficiency and reduce the cost of the operations.
 Coordinate with Product Owners and businesses to provide resolutions for the complex business use cases
 Streamline the product requirements and plan changes in the requirements to support a seamless development cycle
 Take accountability for the successful delivery of the project in all the aspects
 Able to Monitor sprint progress and support the team as required
 Identify dependencies, risk and track is transparently in the tool
 
 Minimum Qualifications: 
 Bachelor’s degree in business or technical field.
 Detail-oriented self-starter with the ability to work independently and drive execution
 Excellent verbal and written communication skills that allow effective communication with both business and technical resources 
 Strong problem-solving, analytic, conceptual, and critical thinking skills
 Minimum 4 years working as an IT business or technical analyst
 Minimum 2 years working on an agile development team
 Ability to breakdown any business needs into user story components 
 Negotiate the scope of the product through business understanding 
 Experience in analyzing multi-dimensional data sets to derive design decisions
 Should be able to mitigate dependencies and risks for deliverables
 Ability to independently plan and execute deliveries 
 Ability to multitask and work on a diverse range of requirements
 Experience in SQL
 Experience in at least one programming language (C#, Java, etc.)
 Should effectively facilitate meetings, brainstorm ideas with the business as well as Development team to derive flawless requirements 
 Experience working with developers
 Experience with Cloud Solutions like Azure, AWS, etc.
 Excellent ability to write details and acceptance criteria for the User Stories with appropriate context.
 Expertise to write Technical Stories to give system and architectural context User requirement 
 Help simplify acceptance tests to automate tests.
 
 
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 
 Experience with Power BI
 Experience with Azure DevOps tool
 Experience SQL Server (MS-SQL, T-SQL), SSIS

jobs.title: Managed Services Analyst
jobs.description:
Essential Functions • Develops and delivers technology services through managed services project activities requiring general knowledge of IQVIA s products and technical expertise. • Responsible for identifying additional product/services opportunities for clients makes decisions within limited parameters under regular supervision. • Has broad understanding of quality standards and work requires minimal revision. • Assists in problem identification, system architecture definition, hardware/software specification and/or design, implementation, testing, client training and solution deployment. • Provides support on Service Requests using the incident management tool, CSM. • Coordinates the routing and handling of Requests For Change to the next level of support under regular supervision. • Escalates issues relating to product support or product operations to the next level of support. • Project management activities include interaction with company and client managers and cost/schedule monitoring.
 Qualifications • Bachelor s Degree Computer Science, a related field, or equivalent experience  Req
 
 IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com 
 
 IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

jobs.title: Sales Reporting & Analysis Analyst
jobs.description:
Mylan Pharmaceuticals Inc. 
 Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life s moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. 
 
 We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. 
 
 
 Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the Sales Reporting & Analysis Analyst role will make an impact:
 
 Key responsibilities for this role include:
 Responsible for calculating Sales Returns and Allowances, Gross Profit, Price Volume Variances, and Royalties on a monthly and quarterly basis which are in accordance to the guidelines of the company’s internal controls, policies, and procedures.
 Performs analytics and develops insight used to build financial models. Develop and build analytical and financial models to identify and address business issues and/or opportunities.
 Streamline the gross-to-net reporting process through increased automation, including use of Alteryx, and leverage visualization techniques to drive financial insights.
 Analyze actual versus forecasted financial results. Provide variance analysis highlighting key business drivers as required.
 Prepare product/customer profitability analysis, including but not limited to, by customer and by product reporting.
 Assist local leadership with financial analysis for special projects.  
 Serve as a resource for internal and external audit requests.
 Serve as a resource in implementing and testing new systems.
 Perform other duties as assigned.
 
 The minimum qualifications for this role are:
 Minimum of a Bachelor s degree (or equivalent) and 0-4 years of experience. However, a combination of experience and/or education will be taken into consideration. 
 Must possess strong finance/accounting technical knowledge. Must have advanced technical skills with Microsoft Excel, Word and PowerPoint and strong system skills, preferably Hyperion and SAP.
 Must have the skill sets to build and maintain sound business relationships. 
 Must have the ability to look beyond the data for assumptions and implications.
 Must demonstrate the ability to take initiative, respond to work demands in a flexible manner and produce timely, high-quality work products in a team environment.
 Must have a proven track record of analyzing business trends, including identification of business risks and opportunities and have the ability to present recommendations in a clear and concise manner.
 Advanced skills in developing financial models, reporting and variance analysis.
 Excellent interpersonal skills and confidence sufficient to interact with all levels of personnel within the organization and the proven ability to lead in a team environment.
 Must possess excellent ability to communicate ideas and analysis effectively to influence business decisions and display confidence and maturity in dealing with all levels of the organization. 
 Must possess strong decision-making capabilities and advanced analytical skills; must be able to see the “big picture”.
 This role may have remote flexibility. The preference is for the role to be based in Morgantown, WV or from a location on the East Coast of the US.
 
 Exact compensation may vary based on items such as skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position is: $41,000 - $93,000. 
 
 At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. 
 
 Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, disability, or protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by local, state, or federal laws, rules, or regulations. 
 
 
#LI-Remote

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Pricing and Contracts
jobs.description:
Mylan Pharmaceuticals Inc. 
 Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life s moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. 
 
 We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. 
 
 
 Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the role will make an impact:
 Work with leadership to understand and develop actionable insights from existing internal and external datasets available throughout the company to inform and drive optimal product strategies.
 Coordinate and build strong working relationships with various internal commercial and technical organizations.
 Leverage creative solutions, analytics, and visualization/reporting techniques to enhance knowledge and drive actionable insights.  This could include use of variance analysis, dashboards, reporting, and automation.
 Contribute to the development and improvement of analytics, internal reporting, and market intelligence reporting.
 Compile large datasets using tools such as Alteryx, PowerBI, AI, Python and SQL, and be able to understand, manipulate and analyze data trends.
 Serve as a subject matter expert on US product portfolios; Monitor product performance by conducting extensive numerical analysis including sales, margin, market share, volume and inventory levels. Lead conversations with other departments to ensure all necessary parties are involved in product strategy discussions. Provide executive summaries for leadership and provide recommendations where needed.
 Support the process of US price change analyses, engaging with other departments as required to create executive summaries for leadership’s review. 
 Lead projects and delegate work in alignment with department objectives.
 Lead the completion of all routine and ad-hoc reporting and analyses per Management’s requests.  Review and address interdepartmental requests for product information.
 Continuously communicate with the Pricing and Contracts and Pricing Analytics cross-functionally to ensure all strategies are clearly defined and on target.
 Provide a first level data check on analysis, reports, and presentations before they are published to other departments or leadership.
 
 The minimum qualifications for this role are:
 Minimum of a Bachelor s degree (or equivalent) and 2-4 years of experience.  However, a combination of experience and/or education will be taken into consideration. 
 Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office, particularly Microsoft Excel.
 Must be able to analyze & visualize data effectively.
 Must possess knowledge of data processing and analysis.
 Experience working with large amounts of data . 
 Generic pharmaceutical industry knowledge preferred.
 Ability to learn the interdependent market variables surrounding the products offered by Viatris’ subsidiaries, and the market channels in which they operate. Ability to perform business analytics relevant to the Pharmaceutical industry.
 Skilled in the areas of product profitability analysis and reporting. Able to assess complex business issues that require collaboration with multiple areas of the business, as well as articulate these business issues to management in a professional manner. Highly analytical, intellectually curious, detail-oriented, and organized. Able to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced, results-oriented business environment. Strong communication and interpersonal skills required.
 Position functions semi-autonomously. Responsibilities may include interviewing, hiring, and training employees; planning, assigning, and directing work; appraising performance; addressing complaints and resolving problems.
 Ability to read and interpret complex business and/or technical documents.  Ability to write comprehensive reports and detailed business correspondence.  Ability to work with groups of people such as other departments and communicate known concepts.  Ability to present to a group of departments.
 Ability to perform computations such as percentages, ratios, and rates. 
 Ability to solve problems with complex variables through non-standardized solutions that require independent judgment and analysis.  Ability to draw inferences and use deductive reasoning with no prescribed procedures to solve complex problems.
 May be required to stand; walk; stoop; bend; kneel; and climb steps. May require use of hands and use of arms. 
 Normal office situation
 Proficiency in speaking, comprehending, reading and writing English is required.
 
 Exact compensation may vary based on skills, experience, and location. The salary range for this position is: $55,000 - $105,000. 
 
 At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. 
 
 Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, disability, or protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by local, state, or federal laws, rules, or regulations.

jobs.title: Analyst
jobs.description:
Job Summary 
Are you passionate about service excellence? Would you love to work for a global organization that is doing more good for people and planet? 
 
 IFF is a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients and health & biosciences, we deliver sustainable innovations that elevate everyday products.
 
 Global Business Services: Delivering streamlined, scalable support—enhancing efficiency, compliance and service excellence across the company.
 
 The role is based in Hyderabad, India(hybrid role). Be part of a creative, solution-oriented team where together we can achieve greatness and make a real impact. Your potential is our inspiration.
 
 Where You’ll Make a Difference 
 Perform daily GR-IR account reconciliations using SAP and Blackline, ensuring timely resolution of open items.
 Monitor GR-IR balances, review aged items, and collaborate with Procurement, Accounts Payable, and Receiving teams to resolve mismatches.
 Prepare and maintain reconciliation documentation for audits, ensuring compliance with internal controls and accounting standards.
 Support internal and external audits by providing reports, explanations, and acting as the focal point for auditor communications.
 Review account reconciliations in Blackline, analyze trends, complete fluctuation analysis, and manage process metrics for transparency.
 Organize monthly briefings with GPOs, implement policy updates, and participate in process improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency.
 Identify and escalate reconciliation issues, perform root cause analysis, and assist in implementing corrective actions.
 Act as the focal point for external financial and statutory filings, ensuring compliance with US GAAP, local accounting principles, and legal requirements.
 Propose and implement practical solutions and new ideas to improve work processes, reduce complexity, and drive continuous improvement.
 
 What Makes You the Right Fit 
 Qualified Accountant (CA, CMA, CPA, CIMA, ACCA) with a bachelor’s degree in commerce.
 0–2 years of experience in GL reconciliation with strong technical accounting skills.
 Proficient in Excel (including basic Macros), Blackline, and Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
 Working knowledge of US GAAP, IFRS, and sound understanding of SAP FICO.
 Strong analytical ability to summarize processes and information clearly and concisely.
 Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with clarity in thought process.
 Ability to share knowledge and collaborate professionally across teams.
 Demonstrates initiative and adaptability to work in a fast-paced environment during peak periods.
 Proactive in proposing ideas and solutions to improve processes and drive efficiency.
 
 How Would You Stand Out? 
 Positive attitude with zeal to learn new things and ability to handle heavy or varied workload.
 Strong in accounting and analytical skills.
 Capable to challenge status quo for process improvement.
 
 Why Choose Us? 
 Hybrid model(3 days/week).
 Two-way transportation.
 Learning and development programs.
 
 
 
 
 We are a global leader in taste, scent, and nutrition, offering our customers a broader range of natural solutions and accelerating our growth strategy. jobs.description:
Job Summary 
Are you passionate about service excellence? Would you love to work for a global organization that is doing more good for people and planet? IFF is a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients and health & biosciences, we deliver sustainable innovations that elevate everyday products. Global Business Services: Delivering streamlined, scalable support—enhancing efficiency, compliance and service excellence across the company. The role is based in Hyderabad, India(hybrid role). Be part of a creative, solution-oriented team where together we can achieve greatness and make a real impact. Your potential is our inspiration. 
 
 Where You’ll Make a Difference
 You will play a critical role in strengthening process excellence, quality, and analytical maturity across Global Business Services (GBS). Your work will directly impact efficiency, cost, quality, and risk outcomes by embedding structured problem‑solving and continuous improvement practices into day‑to‑day operations.
 Specifically, you will:
 Drive measurable and sustainable improvements across GBS processes through Lean Six Sigma initiatives.
 Build organizational capability by training, coaching, and mentoring teams on Quality and CI tools.
 Enable data‑driven decision‑making by owning analytics, performance dashboards, and reporting .
 Reduce waste and cycle time by accelerating digitization and automation using Microsoft Power Platform.
 Strengthen governance by ensuring robust RCA and CAPA execution to prevent issue recurrence.
 
 What Makes You the Right Fit
 You bring a strong mix of continuous improvement expertise, analytical capability, and digital enablement skills , along with hands‑on experience delivering results in a shared services or operational environment.
 You are the right fit if you have:
 Proven 5-8 Years experience leading Lean Six Sigma projects end‑to‑end with quantifiable business benefits.
 Strong working knowledge of Lean, DMAIC, RCA, CAPA, SPC, and basic statistics .
 Advanced Excel and analytics skills , with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
 Hands‑on experience building low‑code solutions using SharePoint, Power Automate, and Power Apps.
 Solid understanding of project management and change management principles.
 A facilitative, coaching mindset with the ability to influence without authority.
 
 How Would You Stand Out?
 You stand out by going beyond project delivery to institutionalize excellence and multiply impact across the organization.
 Differentiators include:
 Designing and delivering high‑impact training programs with demonstrated business outcomes using the Kirkpatrick evaluation model .
 Acting as the QPA/BPI “go‑to” expert for analytics, dashboards, and performance measurement.
 Building scalable CI toolkits, playbooks, and micro‑learning to empower first‑line leaders.
 Ensuring strong control plans, benefits tracking, and CAPA effectiveness validation for sustained results.
 Coaching Green Belts, Yellow Belts, and citizen developers to build internal capability.
 Leveraging process mining and automation insights to proactively shape the CI and digitization roadmap.
 
 Why Choose Us? 
 Training opportunities
 Learning and development programs
 Health insurance
 Hybrid model (3 days working).
